articles.headline: RBI mandates two-factor authentication for all digital payments from April
articles.summary: Starting April 1, the Reserve Bank of India requires all digital payments to be verified using two independent factors, such as a PIN and biometrics. The move aims to curb digital fraud, which rose 194% in value to ₹36,014 crore in FY25. Banks will be held liable for implementing these multi-layer verification systems to ensure transaction security across cards, UPI, and wallets.

articles.headline: PM Modi says India’s AI ecosystem will lead global technological change
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi stated that India’s AI ecosystem is poised to benefit from the country's emerging semiconductor infrastructure. He emphasized that 21st-century India aims to lead global technological change rather than merely witnessing it, positioning the nation as a proactive leader in the evolving digital and manufacturing landscape.

articles.headline: Faster credit reporting to curb loan stacking in digital lending
articles.summary: New RBI rules mandating weekly credit reporting from July 2026 will help digital NBFCs curb 'loan stacking.' Previously, a 30-45 day reporting lag allowed borrowers to take multiple small loans simultaneously. Faster updates will provide lenders with fresher data for risk management. Digital NBFCs currently dominate the personal loan market, accounting for 78% of sanctioned loans by volume.

articles.headline: Nokia warns of rising telecom equipment costs amid component shortages
articles.summary: Telecom gear maker Nokia stated that the West Asia war is exacerbating shortages of components like memory chips, leading to higher costs for wireless equipment. Discussions regarding price increases have begun with Indian telecom operators. Despite these supply chain pressures, Nokia's revenue from India grew 12% in 2025 as the country continues its 4G and 5G network rollouts.

articles.headline: Global helium shortage due to Iran war threatens AI chip production
articles.summary: A halt in natural gas exports from Qatar has triggered a global helium shortage, threatening the production of AI semiconductors and MRI scanners. Helium is essential for cooling chip-making tools. As Qatar provides a third of global supply, prices have doubled on the spot market. Major chip makers in South Korea and Taiwan are scrambling for supplies as inventories begin to dwindle.

articles.headline: Smart scales offer holistic health metrics beyond simple weight tracking
articles.summary: New smart scales are becoming essential screening tools, measuring BMI, body fat, muscle mass, and even vascular health. Devices like the Withings Body Scan provide high accuracy and sync with fitness apps, though entry-level models are also effective for consistent tracking. Experts recommend using these scales to monitor trends and metabolic age as part of a proactive health management routine.

articles.headline: Apple marks 50th anniversary with shift toward AI and services
articles.summary: As Apple reaches its half-century milestone, the company is navigating a transition from hardware-led growth to a recurring-revenue model via services like Apple Music and iCloud. Under Tim Cook, Apple transitioned to internal silicon and is now venturing into spatial computing with Vision Pro. Future growth is tethered to Apple Intelligence and AI partnerships with Google.

articles.headline: Australia mulls action against social media for underage accounts
articles.summary: Australia’s online safety watchdog is considering court action against platforms like TikTok, YouTube, and Meta for failing to keep children under 16 off their services. A compliance report demanded the removal of accounts following a December 10 ban. Despite five million deactivations, many children continue to bypass age assurance systems.

articles.headline: Kaynes’ ₹3,300-cr chip facility a bridge between Sanand and Silicon Valley: PM
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi inaugurated Kaynes Semicon’s ₹3,300 crore facility in Sanand, Gujarat, calling it a 'bridge between Sanand and Silicon Valley'. This marks a key step in India's integration into the global semiconductor supply chain.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry, BEL tie up for ₹1,950-cr mountain radars
articles.summary: The Defence Ministry signed a ₹1,950-crore contract with BEL for two indigenous mountain radars. These will enhance aerial surveillance capabilities along borders in high-altitude regions like J&K and the North-East.

articles.headline: Pricey memory chips hit smartphone sales in 2026
articles.summary: Rising memory component costs and seasonal softness are impacting India's smartphone market, projected to decline 9% in 2026. Premium segments remain resilient, but affordability constraints affect mass-market demand.

articles.headline: CCTV curbs spark supply-chain overhaul, accelerating localisation
articles.summary: Restrictions on non-certified CCTV cameras with foreign-linked components are prompting a structural reset, accelerating localisation efforts. Companies are diversifying sourcing away from China, impacting supply chains.

articles.headline: Eutelsat Negotiates With ISRO to Expand Satellite Launch Options
articles.summary: European satellite internet provider Eutelsat is in talks with ISRO for future launches to diversify beyond SpaceX and Ariane rockets. CEO Jean-François Fallacher noted that India is a strategic market. Eutelsat, which merged with OneWeb in 2023, aims to reach over 1,000 satellites soon and is prepared for India’s growing space economy.

articles.headline: India Recorded 65 Internet Shutdowns in 2025: Report
articles.summary: India imposed 65 internet shutdowns in 2025, ranking among global leaders in connectivity blackouts. Access Now documented 313 shutdowns across 52 countries last year. Common reasons for shutdowns included conflict management, protests, and communal violence, with India frequently using blackouts as an information control tool.

articles.headline: Lava Launches Bold N2 Pro Smartphone in India
articles.summary: Lava has launched the Bold N2 Pro smartphone in India priced at ₹7,999. The device features a 6.75-inch HD+ display with a 120Hz refresh rate and is powered by the UNISOC T7250 chipset. It includes a 50MP rear camera and a 5,000mAh battery, with sales starting April 6 on Flipkart.

articles.headline: Meta Tests Instagram Plus Subscription With Premium Features
articles.summary: Meta is reportedly testing a premium "Instagram Plus" subscription in select regions. The plan includes features such as anonymous Story viewing, rewatch insights, and weekly Story boosts. The initiative targets power users by offering extended Story durations and custom audience list features for a monthly fee.

articles.headline: Apple Releases First Developer Beta of iOS 26.5
articles.summary: Apple has rolled out the first developer beta of iOS 26.5 for iPhones. New features include "Suggested Places" in Apple Maps and end-to-end encryption for RCS messaging. The update aims to refine system performance and introduces several security improvements for the latest hardware models.

articles.headline: AI-Generated Videos Transform Election Campaigns in Southern Indian States
articles.summary: Political parties in Tamil Nadu and Kerala are increasingly using AI-generated videos and deepfakes to target voters. These tools allow for personalized messaging and the creation of content in multiple languages. While enhancing reach, the use of AI has also led to controversies involving deepfakes of rival leaders, prompting calls for stricter monitoring of digital campaign ethics.

articles.headline: 5G Adoption Boosts Indian Monthly Data Usage by 28% in 2025
articles.summary: A report by Nokia reveals that mobile data usage in India surged 28% year-on-year to 27 exabytes in 2025, driven primarily by 5G adoption. Average data usage per user reached 31.1 GB per month. The growth is attributed to the expansion of 5G networks and the availability of affordable handsets. Operators are now focusing on monetizing this traffic through premium 5G services.

articles.headline: Draft IT Rule Tweaks Spark Concerns Over Increased State Oversight
articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and IT (MeitY) has proposed amendments to IT rules that could grant the government expanded oversight over digital platforms. Critics argue the 15-day compliance window is too short and could lead to censorship of news and current affairs content. Industry bodies have warned that the changes might increase legal risks for intermediaries and impact freedom of speech.

articles.headline: Indian Startups Raise Over $10 Billion in FY26 Despite Global Headwinds
articles.summary: Indian startups successfully raised $10.1 billion in the 2025-26 financial year across over 1,000 deals. While overall funding fell compared to previous years, sectors like AI, fintech, and healthtech remained resilient. AI startups were the standout favorites, attracting nearly $2.6 billion. Investors highlighted that while the market has matured, high-quality firms continue to find support.

articles.headline: UPI Processes 219 Billion Transactions Worth ₹285 Lakh Crore in FY26
articles.summary: The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) recorded a massive 219.8 billion transactions in the last fiscal year, with a total value of ₹284.7 lakh crore. Monthly transaction volumes remained consistently above 20 billion. This represents a 41.7% increase in volume, cementing UPI's position as the dominant digital payment method in India. PhonePe, Google Pay, and Paytm maintain market leadership.

articles.headline: PM Modi Inaugurates Kaynes Semiconductor Assembly Plant in Sanand
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has inaugurated the Kaynes Semi-con plant in Sanand, Gujarat, marking a milestone for the India Semiconductor Mission. The facility will focus on the assembly, testing, and packaging of chips for the domestic and global markets. This is the second semiconductor unit to be approved under the central incentive scheme, aimed at reducing dependence on imports.

articles.headline: Oracle to Lay Off Nearly 12,000 Employees in India Operations
articles.summary: US-based IT giant Oracle has reportedly laid off approximately 12,000 employees in India as part of a global restructuring that will see 30,000 jobs cut worldwide. The move is part of the company's plan to streamline operations and pivot more aggressively toward AI-driven cloud services. Impacted employees are primarily in technical and support roles across major Indian hubs.

articles.headline: X to HC: Govt bid to block 12 accounts is disproportionate
articles.summary: X Corp has told the Delhi High Court that the Indian government's order to block 12 accounts for allegedly spreading misinformation is 'disproportionate.' The social media giant argued that the move violates freedom of speech and that the government failed to provide specific reasons for the block under the IT Act. The court has scheduled further hearings for next week.

articles.headline: Global economy faces damage as US-Iran conflict intensifies in West Asia
articles.summary: The conflict between the US and Iran is escalating following a heavy bunker-buster strike on an Iranian ammunition dump. While US President Donald Trump suggests he may end the war even if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, experts warn that the global economy and energy supplies will suffer significantly regardless of the war's eventual resolution and damage control is essential.

articles.headline: Trump willing to end war without reopening the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: President Donald Trump has told aides he is willing to wind down the military campaign against Iran even if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. The administration aims to achieve core goals of hobbling Iran's navy while pressuring Tehran diplomatically for a later reopening. This shift suggests a willingness to tolerate economic disruption to avoid a prolonged ground and naval mission.

articles.headline: Russian tanker docks in Cuba, testing Trump’s fuel blockade limits
articles.summary: A Russian tanker carrying 730,000 barrels of oil reached Cuba, providing brief relief to the island's fuel-starved economy. The arrival breaks a two-month blockade imposed by the Trump administration. While Trump says he has 'no problem' with survival-level fuel, the US is cases-by-case allowing some shipments to support Cuba's private sector while bypassing state-controlled entities.

articles.headline: Indian trade delegation visits China for first time in five years
articles.summary: A delegation from the PHD Chamber of Commerce and Industry is visiting Shanghai and Jiangsu, marking the first such trade mission since bilateral relations were frozen in 2020. The visit follows the normalization of relations last year. Business leaders held roundtables to explore collaboration opportunities, signaling a cautious revival of commercial ties between the two major economies.

articles.headline: Trump slams allies and Iran seeks guarantees for truce
articles.summary: President Donald Trump expressed frustration with allies unwilling to support the US war effort, telling them to secure their own oil. Meanwhile, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian stated Tehran is willing to end the conflict if guarantees prevent further aggression. The IRGC has threatened to target US tech companies, including Microsoft and Tesla, in retaliation for regional attacks.

articles.headline: Analysis: Iran retains military ability to retaliate and resist
articles.summary: Despite US-Israeli claims that Iran’s military is 'neutralized', data suggests Tehran remains a capable foe. While ballistic missile strikes have decreased, Iran is deploying harder-to-intercept low-flying drones. Experts suggest Iran's strategy is to cause sustained economic pain and drive up costs for the US, while withholding enough arsenal to maintain long-term leverage.

articles.headline: Ukrainian drone strikes hit Russia's Baltic oil port
articles.summary: Ukrainian drones struck Russia’s Baltic Sea port of Ust-Luga, hitting an oil loading terminal. This marks the fifth attack on the facility in ten days, complicating Russia's crude oil exports. Ukrainian forces have intensified strikes on Russian energy infrastructure over the past month as the conflict continues.

articles.headline: India faces pressure as US seeks plurilateral e-comm moratorium
articles.summary: The US plans to pursue a plurilateral e-commerce duties moratorium after Brazil and Turkey blocked its extension at the WTO. India may need to align with these nations to safeguard its policy space.

articles.headline: Developing countries assert roles at WTO MC14
articles.summary: Developing countries and LDCs asserted themselves at the WTO MC14, ensuring discussions remained open, fair, and inclusive. They aim to move beyond being 'rule-takers' in international forums.

articles.headline: Iran sets giant oil tanker ablaze off Dubai after Trump warnings
articles.summary: Tehran attacked and set ablaze a fully-loaded oil tanker off Dubai. US President Trump warned countries to find 'courage' to secure the Strait of Hormuz, amidst rising regional tensions and threats against US companies.

articles.headline: WTO needs to rediscover its raison d’etre
articles.summary: The WTO's relevance is questioned amidst global trade disruptions. India's stance against extending the e-commerce moratorium and resisting the IFD proposal highlights its need for policy space.

articles.headline: US Job Openings Fell in February as Hiring Slowed
articles.summary: US job vacancies decreased to 6.88 million in February from 7.24 million in January, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics. The slowdown in hiring occurred just before the Iran war triggered new economic uncertainty. Rising oil prices now threaten to increase operating costs, potentially impeding future labor demand.

articles.headline: Euro Zone Inflation Jumps to 2.5% on War Driven Energy Costs
articles.summary: The euro area saw inflation rise to 2.5% in March, the steepest jump since 2022, driven by energy costs following the Iran war. This heightens expectations for European Central Bank interest rate hikes. While core inflation slowed to 2.3%, services gauge easing was offset by volatile energy prices hitting 15-year yield peaks in German bonds.

articles.headline: King Charles to Visit US Despite Diplomatic Friction Over Iran
articles.summary: King Charles III will proceed with a state visit to the US in late April to celebrate the 250th anniversary of American Independence. The visit goes ahead despite President Trump’s criticism of the UK government for not supporting the war in Iran. The trip follows Trump's 2025 visit to the UK intended to shore up the special relationship.

articles.headline: Japan Deploys First Long Range Missiles with 1,000 KM Range
articles.summary: Japan operationalized its first long-range Type-12 missiles at a southwestern army camp. The upgraded missiles can hit targets up to 1,000 kilometers away, potentially reaching mainland China. Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi cited the most severe postwar security environment as the reason for bolstering Japan's offensive and deterrence capabilities.

articles.headline: Russian Oil Tanker Arrives in Cuba Amid Severe Energy Crisis
articles.summary: The Russian vessel Anatoly Kolodkin docked in Cuba carrying 730,000 barrels of oil, the first such delivery in three months. The US allowed the shipment despite its energy blockade. Cuba produces only 40% of its required fuel and is suffering from food and medicine shortages and extensive blackouts during its deepest economic crisis.

articles.headline: Nine Militants Killed After Attack on Pakistan School
articles.summary: Pakistan security forces killed nine militants who stormed a school in Khyber district and hoisted their own flag. Forces reinstated the national flag after a fierce gun battle. The militants had attempted to seize control of the building in the northwestern province before being neutralized in a swift response by security units.

articles.headline: US Warns Iran That Next Few Days Will Be Decisive
articles.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth warned that the conflict with Iran would intensify unless a deal is reached. The warning follows Iran's drone attack on an oil tanker off Dubai. In response, Iran's IRGC threatened to target 18 US companies, including Apple and Google, in the region starting April 1 as retaliation for US-led attacks.

articles.headline: Italy Denies Use of Sicily Airbase for US War Operations
articles.summary: Italy refused permission for US bombers to land at Sigonella air base before missions in West Asia. Reports suggest authorization was denied because the US failed to consult Italian military leadership as required by treaties. Italy's decision comes amid rising regional tensions following the US-led strikes against Iran.

articles.headline: Israel to Establish Security Buffer Zone in Southern Lebanon
articles.summary: Israel’s Defense Minister Israel Katz announced the military will control southern Lebanon up to the Litani River at the end of operations against Hezbollah. The "security zone" aims to eliminate Radwan forces. Residents evacuated from the area will be barred from returning until northern Israel’s safety is guaranteed by the destruction of weaponry.

articles.headline: China and Pakistan Issue Joint Call for Immediate Ceasefire
articles.summary: China and Pakistan released a five-point joint proposal for an immediate ceasefire in the Iran war. They called for the protection of vessels in the Strait of Hormuz and the restoration of normal navigation. The proposal follows talks in Beijing between foreign ministers aimed at stabilizing global energy supplies hobbled by the conflict.

articles.headline: H-1B Visa Selection Rates Jump to 50% for FY27 Season
articles.summary: The H-1B visa selection rate for the upcoming fiscal year has surged to approximately 50%, a significant increase from previous years. US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) attributed the rise to new regulations that prevent multiple registrations for the same individual. This change has benefited master's degree holders and those already residing in the US on other work permits.

articles.headline: Israeli Military Prepared to Continue Fighting for Weeks in Gaza
articles.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated that the military is prepared to continue its operations in Gaza for several more weeks to achieve its security objectives. The announcement comes amid growing international calls for a ceasefire and a deepening humanitarian crisis. Netanyahu emphasized that the goal remains the total dismantling of hostile infrastructure near Israel's borders.

articles.headline: Go to Hormuz and take your oil: Trump to allies
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump urged allies to secure their own oil from the Persian Gulf, stating the US won't protect the region for others. This follows US fuel prices hitting $100 per barrel. He suggested allies build up their own 'delayed courage' and stop relying on American taxpayers to secure global supply lines.

articles.headline: US strikes Isfahan, home to Iran's main nuclear sites
articles.summary: US drones hit the central Iranian city of Isfahan, targeting infrastructure near nuclear research facilities. The attack aimed to degrade Iran's ability to develop weapons-grade fuel. While Iran claimed its air defenses successfully repelled the strike, satellite images suggested significant damage to research infrastructure in the highly sensitive region.

articles.headline: Iran threatens to hit US tech giants if sanctions continue
articles.summary: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard warned it would target major US tech companies including Microsoft, Google, and Apple if the US does not halt its 'destructive' actions and sanctions. The warning cited the use of American technology in targeting Iranian leaders and promised 'retaliation in kind' against global digital infrastructure associated with the United States.

articles.headline: Sensex skids 7.1% in FY26 as India becomes global market laggard
articles.summary: India's equity market emerged as a global underperformer in FY26, with the Sensex dropping 7.1%, its deepest rout since the pandemic. This slump contrasts with double-digit gains in global peers like South Korea's Kospi (112.7%) and Japan's Nikkei (45.7%). The decline reflects regional pressures and capital outflows even as other international indices posted significant rallies.

articles.headline: RBI monitors corporate treasuries to assess arbitrage trades and defend rupee
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is seeking data on corporate treasury positions to identify large arbitrage trades as it fights to defend the rupee from capital outflows. The rupee hit a record low of 95.1250 against the dollar before recovery. The central bank recently capped banks' net open positions at $100 million to curb volatility and discourage speculative currency trading.

articles.headline: Stockbrokers ask government to defer hike in securities transaction tax
articles.summary: The Association of NSE Members of India (ANMI) plans to request a delay in the proposed STT hike for equity futures and options. The lobby cites the current market rout induced by the Iran war and falling volumes as reasons for the deferment. The government previously proposed raising STT on futures to 0.05% and on options to 0.15% starting April 1.

articles.headline: Broad-based sell-off hits all Indian market sectors in FY26
articles.summary: Indian equities saw a cross-sector downturn in FY26, with real estate and IT emerging as the worst performers. The BSE Realty index fell 23.6%, while IT dropped 22.7% due to slowing global spend and AI-led disruptions. Defensive sectors like FMCG also declined by 13.7%. Metals were a rare gainer, rising 19.4% on firm commodity prices despite the general market pessimism.

articles.headline: NBFCs see resilient credit demand despite global economic turmoil
articles.summary: Non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) in India expect to maintain credit growth of 16-17% despite the US-Iran war. Industry leaders noted that NBFCs provide critical last-mile connectivity to millions of small businesses that still lack formal credit access. While some pressure may emerge in rural income, the fundamental demand for credit remains robust across the emerging economy.

articles.headline: West Asia war raises airfares as airlines face triple cost whammy
articles.summary: Airfares on international and domestic routes have surged up to 30% due to rising fuel prices, airspace closures, and the removal of fare caps. Travellers are being advised to book refundable tickets and remain flexible. High-traffic leisure routes are seeing significant hikes as airlines pass on fuel surcharges of up to ₹2,300 to manage operational constraints caused by the conflict.

articles.headline: CAG report flags rising debt and revenue shortfall in Telangana
articles.summary: A CAG report for FY2024-25 revealed that Telangana's total outstanding debt reached ₹4.47 lakh crore. The state faced a significant revenue shortfall, realizing only 76% of estimated receipts. The report highlighted under-utilization of the budget and increased interest payment burdens, with the state relying heavily on open market borrowings to bridge fiscal deficits.

articles.headline: EU Parliament to scrutinize India-EU free trade agreement
articles.summary: Angelika Niebler, chair of the EU delegation for relations with India, stated that the European Parliament will closely scrutinize the India-EU FTA to ensure a balanced approach. While agriculture is largely excluded, environmental standards and carbon regulations remain key focus areas. The pact is expected to enter into force next year as both sides seek resilient partnerships.

articles.headline: Asian nations turn to fuel bartering amid Middle East conflict
articles.summary: Indonesia, Japan, and Vietnam are exploring energy bartering and alternative suppliers like Russia to offset fuel shortages caused by the war in West Asia. The Philippines has declared a national energy emergency, while Sri Lanka has rationed fuel. China’s ban on refined fuel exports has further tightened the regional market, forcing nations to seek urgent supplies.

articles.headline: Indian diesel exports to Southeast Asia reach seven-year high
articles.summary: India's diesel exports to Southeast Asia surged in March as refiners, led by Reliance Industries, covered supply gaps caused by the West Asian war. Traders pivoted to India as a swing supplier after Middle East and Chinese supplies were disrupted. The surge in exports is helping ease regional tightness despite the reinstatement of domestic export taxes.

articles.headline: Income Tax department notifies all seven ITR forms for AY27
articles.summary: The Income Tax department has notified all ITR forms for the assessment year 2026-27, enabling taxpayers to file returns for income earned in FY2025-26. The forms cater to individuals, HUFs, and businesses, with Sahaj (ITR-1) and Sugam (ITR-4) remaining the simplified options for small taxpayers. The deadline for non-audit cases is July 31.

articles.headline: Unrealised tax demands rise to ₹38 lakh crore by FY25-end
articles.summary: Unrealised tax demands, both direct and indirect, have surged to ₹38 lakh crore by FY25-end. Direct tax demands grew at a CAGR of 26% between FY20-FY25, suggesting potential overestimations by tax authorities.

articles.headline: India may pivot to US as W. Asia conflict hits LPG availability
articles.summary: Due to the West Asia conflict impacting LPG production, India is expected to increase NGL imports from the US, which hit a record 139,000 b/d in 2025. US exporters may increase butane in their cargo mix.

articles.headline: CAD rises, rupee trajectory fragile
articles.summary: India's Current Account Deficit (CAD) rise and fragile rupee trajectory are linked to oil price shocks, dollar strength, and thinner capital inflows. The rupee's adjustment towards fair value is slow, making it sensitive to external shocks.

articles.headline: For a permanent ‘stabilisation fund’
articles.summary: An Economic Stabilisation Fund is proposed, institutionalised by statute with States as stakeholders. This fund aims to provide a buffer against unforeseen economic disruptions, insulating growth trajectory.

articles.headline: NOP cap to provide relief to ₹; external risks remain
articles.summary: The RBI's cap on banks' Net Open Position (NOP) to $100 million aims to curb speculative dollar activity. This could lead to rupee recovery, but geopolitical risks and a strong dollar sustain upward pressure.

articles.headline: More global investors exited gold ETFs in March
articles.summary: Global investors withdrew $2.34 for every $1 invested in gold ETFs in March, according to WGC data. Fears of inflation, rate hikes, and dollar strength, alongside bank offloading reserves, drove the net exodus.

articles.headline: Commerce Secretary: WTO MC14 Shows Multilateralism Remains Relevant
articles.summary: Union Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal stated that MC14 in Cameroon highlights the relevance of multilateralism. He noted that developing nations are no longer just "rule takers." While consensus remained elusive on the ecommerce Customs duty moratorium, discussions will continue in Geneva to ensure a fair and inclusive global trade environment.

articles.headline: Rupee May Fall to 97 Per Dollar Amid West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: A Business Standard poll suggests the rupee could slide to 97 against the dollar if the West Asia war prolongs. The currency saw its sharpest annual decline since 2011-12 in FY26, dropping nearly 10% due to capital outflows and high crude prices. Experts anticipate a modest recovery to 96 per dollar by the end of June if tensions ease.

articles.headline: India Russia Oil Imports Hit Nine Month High in March
articles.summary: India's Russian crude imports surged to 1.96 million barrels per day in March as West Asian supplies tightened due to regional war. The rise followed a 30-day US waiver on Moscow's oil purchases. Simultaneously, imports from Iraq and Saudi Arabia fell sharply, while Angola emerged as a major alternative supplier for Indian refiners.

articles.headline: Govt Cancels Auction of 11 Critical and Strategic Mineral Blocks
articles.summary: The government annulled the auction of 11 critical mineral blocks due to poor investor response and a lack of technically-qualified bidders. The cancellation affects blocks in West Bengal and other regions. This is a setback for domestic production efforts aimed at securing supply chains for minerals essential for high-tech industries.

articles.headline: West Asia War Could Erode India GDP by 1 Percentage Point
articles.summary: An EY report warns that India’s FY27 GDP growth could drop by 1 percentage point if the West Asia conflict persists. Retail inflation could rise by 1.5 percentage points from baseline estimates. Sectors like textiles, paints, and chemicals are at high risk, with potential reductions in employment and incomes dampening aggregate demand.

articles.headline: Indian Business Delegation Visits China After Five Year Hiatus
articles.summary: A delegation from the PHD Chamber of Commerce and Industry is visiting Shanghai and Jiangsu, marking the first trade visit to China since 2020. This follows the normalization of bilateral relations last year. The visit aims to restore economic interactions after a five-year freeze following the military standoff at Eastern Ladakh.

articles.headline: Regulated Gas Price for ONGC and Oil India Raised to $7
articles.summary: The government increased the administered price mechanism (APM) gas price for ONGC and Oil India to $7 per mmBtu, up from $6.75. This revision applies to production from legacy fields, which accounts for 60% of domestic gas production. The hike is expected to impact costs for user industries, including fertilizer and piped cooking gas.

articles.headline: Indian States Utilised Only 55% of Capex Budget Till February
articles.summary: Analysis of CAG data shows 22 Indian states utilized only 55.27% of their combined ₹10.22 trillion budgeted capital expenditure in the first 11 months of FY26. Haryana and Himachal Pradesh led in spending, while West Bengal and Meghalaya were among the lowest. In contrast, Central government capex utilization stood at 84.8% of revised estimates.

articles.headline: RBI Net Short Dollar Position Hit $77 Billion in February
articles.summary: The RBI's outstanding net short dollar position in the rupee forward market rose to $77.25 billion by the end of February, up from $68.42 billion in January. The increase was driven by longer-tenure contracts. The data reflects the central bank's strategy to manage currency stability amid persistent global market uncertainties.

articles.headline: MGNREGA Household Work in FY26 Lowest in Six Years
articles.summary: Preliminary data shows 53.2 million households received MGNREGA work in FY26, the lowest since FY20. Average work per household fell to 42.9 days. The scheme is set for replacement by the Viksit Bharat Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission in FY27. Budget allocation for the transition includes ₹30,000 crore for MGNREGA residues.

articles.headline: Brokers Get Three Month Relief on Capital Market Exposure Rules
articles.summary: The RBI deferred the rollout of capital market exposure norms until July 1 to provide stockbrokers time to address operational challenges. The rules involve financing proprietary trading and higher equity collateral haircuts. Brokers are expected to use the window to seek further relaxations regarding proprietary desk leverage and profit margins.

articles.headline: Silver Funds Correct 14% Following Massive One Year Rally
articles.summary: Silver funds corrected 14.1% over the past month after a 121.6% bull run. Experts say investors should reset expectations as profit booking and a stronger dollar weigh on prices. Despite the dip, structural demand from AI data centres and green technology remains intact, with the metal likely to consolidate between $60-80 in the near term.

articles.headline: FIIs Pull Out ₹1.6 Lakh Crore While DIIs Pump in ₹8.5 Lakh Crore
articles.summary: Foreign institutional investors (FIIs) withdrew over ₹1.6 lakh crore from Indian equities in FY26, the highest ever in a financial year. Conversely, domestic institutional investors (DIIs) provided a record ₹8.5 lakh crore backstop. The exodus was driven by regional geopolitical tensions and high valuations, though the rupee's 10% slide against the dollar also weighed on sentiment.

articles.headline: RBI Caps Loans for Share Purchases and IPOs at ₹1 Crore
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has limited individual loans for purchasing shares and other securities to ₹1 crore per borrower. This cap applies to IPO subscriptions, Esops, and secondary market purchases. The move aims to curb speculative excessive borrowing and systemic credit risk during market booms, ensuring individual leverage remains within sustainable limits.

articles.headline: Vegetable and Wheat Prices Crash Following Record Supply Glut
articles.summary: Vegetable prices in India have plunged since January, with potatoes down 40%, onions 50%, and tomatoes up to 80% in some wholesale markets. The drop is attributed to a massive supply glut and peak harvest arrivals. While a relief for consumers, the price crash has triggered farm distress in states like Maharashtra, Gujarat, and Madhya Pradesh as farmers struggle to recover costs.

articles.headline: Indian Rupee Finishes FY26 as Worst Performing Currency in Asia
articles.summary: The Indian rupee ended the financial year at 94.83 against the US dollar, marking a 9.88% annual loss. It was the worst performer among 10 Asian peer currencies, trailing the Japanese yen and Philippine peso. Capital outflows from Indian equities and a global scramble for dollar-backed assets amid geopolitical tensions put persistent pressure on the currency throughout the year.

articles.headline: Shipping Rates Jump 40% as Fuel and Insurance Costs Surge
articles.summary: International shipping rates have spiked by up to 40% as carriers adjust to higher fuel costs and war-risk insurance premiums linked to the West Asia conflict. Major lines like Maersk and MSC have implemented new surcharges on routes connecting India to Europe and the Mediterranean. This second round of increases in a month is expected to impact the competitiveness of Indian exports.

articles.headline: India Records 219 Advance Pricing Pacts with CBDT in FY26
articles.summary: The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) signed a record 219 Advance Pricing Agreements (APAs) in the 2025-26 financial year. These agreements provide tax certainty to multinational corporations on their cross-border transactions. Out of these, 84 were bilateral pacts with countries like the US and UK. The program aims to reduce litigation and foster a more stable tax environment in India.

articles.headline: Foreign Portfolio Investment in Indian G-Secs Falls Sharply in FY26
articles.summary: Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) bought only ₹3,546 crore worth of Indian government securities in FY26, a sharp decline from ₹2.31 lakh crore in the previous year. The drop is attributed to a global shift toward safer US bonds amid rising West Asia tensions and a weakening rupee. Analysts suggest that significant inflows are unlikely without further interest rate hikes by the RBI.

articles.headline: War clouds impact Gulf job plans for over 1 lakh workers
articles.summary: Rising tensions in West Asia have stalled recruitment for over 100,000 Indian workers. Recruitment agencies report a complete halt in visa processing for sectors like hospitality, construction, and healthcare. Families are increasingly concerned as the labor export sector, which contributes significant remittances to India's economy, faces its worst disruption in years.

articles.headline: Property tax haul crosses ₹7,500 crore as BMC hits target
articles.summary: The BMC collected ₹7,534 crore in property tax for the 2025-26 fiscal year, exceeding its revised target by 2%. The collection is a record high for the municipal body, driven by an increase in residential units and stricter enforcement. However, some civic groups criticized the lack of exemptions for smaller flats despite previous political promises.

articles.headline: Govt keeps Ready Reckoner rates unchanged in push for realty
articles.summary: The Maharashtra government has decided to keep Ready Reckoner rates unchanged for the 2026-27 fiscal year to support the real estate sector. The move is intended to maintain stability and boost homebuyer confidence amidst high interest rates. Developers welcomed the decision, stating it will help keep property prices affordable for middle-class buyers.

articles.headline: 86% in India report workplace disruptions, above global levels
articles.summary: A new survey found that 86% of Indian employees reported significant workplace disruptions last year, much higher than the global average of 61%. Factors such as rapid AI integration and shifting work cultures were cited as primary drivers. The report emphasizes the need for urgent upskilling, as nearly 90% of the workforce feels the need to learn new skills.

articles.headline: Weak planning and fragmented governance hinder productivity in Indian cities
articles.summary: A report by Janaagraha reveals that while Indian cities are expanding, weak planning and fragmented governance are holding back productivity. India's per capita income remains behind G20 peers despite high urbanization. Productivity increases by only 12% when an Indian city doubles its urbanization, compared to 19% in China, due to unplanned sprawl and lack of empowered local bodies.

articles.headline: India plans tender for 3,000 electric buses by June 2026
articles.summary: The Centre is set to launch a tender for over 3,000 e-buses under the PM e-Bus Sewa Scheme to reduce fuel dependence. This follows a major tender for 10,000 units earlier this year. India aims to deploy 50,000 electric buses to modernize public transport. E-bus sales rose to 4,978 units in FY26, and annual volumes are projected to exceed 17,000 units by FY27.

articles.headline: Indian real estate sector requires massive formal capital to scale
articles.summary: Top executives at the Mint India Investment Summit emphasized that India's real estate market needs significant institutional funding to meet growing demand. While the global sector is worth $60 trillion, India's base is only $0.5 trillion. Consolidation is pushing developers toward private credit and formal equity as they expand into urban housing, office spaces, and hospitality.

articles.headline: Global sulphur shortage may disrupt BHEL boiler manufacturing output
articles.summary: A shortage of sulphur linked to the West Asia conflict threatens to slow down boiler production at BHEL. Sulphur is essential for welding consumables and industrial gases used in manufacturing boiler components. BHEL is currently dipping into critical emergency stocks as its thermal power plant order book swells to over ₹76,000 crore following a revival in the thermal power sector.

articles.headline: Adani Green taps external contractors to meet 50GW capacity goal
articles.summary: Adani Green Energy is shifting away from its exclusive in-house EPC model, hiring external contractors like L&T and Sterling and Wilson to speed up projects. The company needs to add 7.75GW annually to reach its 2030 target of 50GW. The strategy aims to leverage external bandwidth and expertise as it develops the massive 30GW renewable energy complex at Khavda, Gujarat.

articles.headline: India accelerates piped gas rollout amid West Asia LPG disruptions
articles.summary: The government added over 310,000 piped natural gas (PNG) connections in March to counter LPG supply disruptions from the West Asia war. While 60% of LPG is imported through the blocked Strait of Hormuz, half of India's natural gas is produced locally. Piped gas is being pushed as a more reliable and convenient alternative for domestic and commercial kitchens during the energy crisis.

articles.headline: Modest uptake for Delhi Jal Board water bill penalty waiver
articles.summary: The Delhi Jal Board's penalty waiver scheme has seen limited participation since October, recovering only ₹550 crore against total dues exceeding ₹80,000 crore. Residents and stakeholders attribute the poor response to disputes over inflated billing and the frequent rollout of waiver schemes, which they argue discourages regular payments among consumers.

articles.headline: Centre clears ₹1,561 crore for Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0
articles.summary: The Union government released ₹1,561.53 crore to five states—Uttar Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Odisha, and Maharashtra—under the revamped Jal Jeevan Mission. The funding follows new agreements focused on service delivery outcomes and stricter validation. The mission aims to provide doorstep tap-water connections to nearly 194 million rural households.

articles.headline: Colonial-era 'jinxed' Delhi CM bungalow to be demolished
articles.summary: A colonial-era bungalow at 33, Sham Nath Marg, which has remained largely unoccupied for over two decades due to its reputation for being 'jinxed', is set for demolition. Officials announced that the site will be replaced by a new office complex, ending its long vacancy in political and administrative circles.

articles.headline: PM Modi inaugurates semiconductor facility and Gujarat infra projects
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated Kaynes Technology’s semiconductor plant in Sanand, Gujarat, as part of India’s self-reliance push. He also dedicated infrastructure projects worth ₹20,000 crore, including the Ahmedabad-Dholera Expressway. Modi emphasized that India’s semiconductor market is projected to cross $100 billion by the end of the decade.

articles.headline: PNG Drive 2.0 accelerated due to war, extended till June 5
articles.summary: The government has extended the Piped Natural Gas (PNG) Drive 2.0 till June 5 due to the West Asia conflict. City gas companies have added over three lakh PNG connections, with 2.67 lakh more applications in March.

articles.headline: Govt to step up hydropower capacity expansion by 2035
articles.summary: The government plans to accelerate hydropower capacity expansion to 2035, especially in J&K, with the Indus Waters Treaty in abeyance. This includes incentivising smaller projects and expanding private investment.

articles.headline: Govt Extends Piped Gas Drive and Adds 310,000 Connections
articles.summary: The National Piped Natural Gas (PNG) drive has been extended by three months until June 30 to accelerate adoption. India added 310,000 PNG connections in March. To ensure cooking fuel supply amid LPG shortages, select petrol pumps have been allowed to distribute kerosene, and gas companies have waived security deposits for new users.

articles.headline: ACME and NTPC Ink Pacts to Supply Green Ammonia
articles.summary: ACME Cleantech, NTPC, and others signed 10-year agreements to supply green ammonia to fertilizer companies at prices between ₹49.75/kg and ₹64.74/kg. Under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, ACME will supply 370,000 tonnes annually to major firms including IFFCO and Coromandel International to support the transition to green fuel.

articles.headline: UP Government to Invest ₹2,200 Crore in Jharkhand Coal Block
articles.summary: The Uttar Pradesh cabinet approved an investment of over ₹2,200 crore for a coal mining block in Jharkhand to feed the Ghatampur Thermal Power Station. The Pachwara South block, funded via a 70:30 debt-equity mix, is expected to start coal extraction in August. The move aims to reduce power generation costs and ensure uninterrupted supply.

articles.headline: Smaller Aircraft Manufacturing Expected to Thrive Under Udan 2.0 Scheme
articles.summary: Civil Aviation Minister Ram Mohan Naidu stated that a six-fold budget increase for regional connectivity will boost smaller aircraft manufacturing in India. Major global firms like Embraer and Leonardo are exploring local assembly lines. The government plans to develop 100 new airports and 200 heli-pads over the next decade to enhance last-mile connectivity for smaller towns.

articles.headline: Indian Railways Modernizes Parcel Policy with Longer Lease Contracts
articles.summary: Indian Railways has liberalized its parcel policy, offering long-term (three-year) lease contracts to provide more predictable commercial terms for users. The new norms allow for two extensions of one year each and permit up to 10% overloading with a nominal fee. The move is designed to encourage greater participation from large aggregators and enhance the efficiency of rail cargo services.

articles.headline: Diesel supply worries rise for farmers as rabi crops reach harvest
articles.summary: Farmers in north India are concerned that a ban on loose diesel sales, intended to curb illegal storage, will disrupt the narrow wheat harvest window. Agriculture accounts for 40% of India's annual diesel demand. Stakeholders warn that any disruption in fuel supply for heavy machinery could impact crop quality, delay procurement, and hit overall farm incomes during the peak season.

articles.headline: Colleges switch to firewood and food apps amid LPG cylinder shortage
articles.summary: The blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has triggered a severe LPG shortage in India, forcing educational institutions to adopt drastic measures. Some universities have shut campuses and moved to online classes, while others have switched to diesel burners or firewood for hostel kitchens. Commercial kitchens are currently at the back of the priority queue for gas supplies behind households.

articles.headline: Centre releases ₹1,500 crore in grants to six states for rural governance
articles.summary: The finance ministry has disbursed over ₹1,500 crore to Telangana, Rajasthan, Maharashtra, Uttarakhand, Mizoram, and Meghalaya as part of the 15th Finance Commission grants. The funds include tied grants for sanitation and water supply and untied grants for local development. These installments aim to enhance the fiscal autonomy of rural local bodies for the final year of the award period.

articles.headline: Techie couple from Telangana die by suicide in Bengaluru
articles.summary: A 31-year-old woman allegedly jumped to her death from the 17th floor of her Bengaluru apartment after finding her 32-year-old husband dead inside. Police believe the husband died by suicide following domestic disputes and marital discord. Both were tech professionals originally from Siddipet, Telangana. Separate cases of unnatural death have been registered.

articles.headline: Thirty-five Maoists surrender in Bastar as elimination deadline ends
articles.summary: Thirty-five Maoist cadres surrendered in Chhattisgarh's Bastar region just hours before the Union government's March 31 deadline to eradicate Left Wing Extremism. Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai declared the state now 'free' of Naxalism. Security forces also recovered weapons and valuables worth over ₹11 crore from Maoist dumps in recent operations in Bijapur.

articles.headline: Drugs worth ₹5.14 crore seized in Bengaluru; 11 arrested
articles.summary: Bengaluru police arrested 11 individuals, including two foreign nationals, and seized banned substances valued at approximately ₹5.14 crore. The arrests followed specific inputs regarding drug sales in multiple areas of the city. Cases have been registered under the NDPS Act, and investigations are ongoing.

articles.headline: Woman passenger injured by stone pelting on Kerala train
articles.summary: A 22-year-old woman, Aiswarya Ramakrishnan, sustained serious facial injuries and lost three teeth after unidentified persons pelted stones at the Kannur Executive Express near Kozhikode. The victim was seated by a window in an unreserved compartment when the incident occurred. Railway police have registered a case and are investigating to trace the perpetrators.

articles.headline: NCST directs joint survey of displaced tribals in Telugu states
articles.summary: The National Commission for Scheduled Tribes (NCST) suggested a joint survey by Chhattisgarh, Andhra Pradesh, and Telangana to settle the fate of 32,000 displaced Adivasis. These 'Internally Displaced People' fled Maoist violence decades ago. The commission recommended giving them the option to return to birth villages with land allotments or securing their rights where they currently reside.

articles.headline: Surrendered Maoists claim movement failed to evolve with time
articles.summary: Top surrendered Naxals in Bastar stated that the CPI(Maoist) became an anachronism by failing to negotiate from strength in the late 2000s. They cited the strengthening of state intelligence and the breaching of their Abujhmad bastion as turning points. The former commanders highlighted a leadership vacuum and a communication gap that led to the movement's eventual decline.

articles.headline: CAG report flags systemic failures in Odisha tribal welfare
articles.summary: A CAG audit revealed that 54% of Odisha’s Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) are excluded from welfare coverage. The report highlighted non-functional assets, including 55 nutritional centers and various drinking water projects. It also flagged MGNREGS failures, where only a small fraction of households received the full 100 days of employment between 2019 and 2024.

articles.headline: Three family members killed in Mirzapur road accident
articles.summary: Three members of a single family died when their motorcycle was hit by a trailer truck on the Chunar-Jamui road in Uttar Pradesh's Mirzapur district. The accident occurred under the jurisdiction of the Ahraura police station. Police are investigating the incident.

articles.headline: Air India Express flight makes emergency landing in Lucknow
articles.summary: A Delhi-bound Air India Express flight from Bagdogra made an emergency landing at Lucknow airport after the crew detected smoke in the aircraft. Officials confirmed that the plane was diverted safely and all passengers are out of danger.

articles.headline: Six States take action on shortfall in JJM projects
articles.summary: Six States have taken action, including imposing penalties, for financial irregularities and shoddy work under the Jal Jeevan Mission. Recoveries have been made through liquidated damages and forfeiture of deposits.

articles.headline: Centre Appoints Vir Vikram Yadav as New DGCA Chief
articles.summary: Vir Vikram Yadav has been appointed the new Director General of Civil Aviation, replacing Faiz Ahmed Kidwai. Kidwai has been transferred to the Department of Personnel and Training as additional secretary. The leadership change comes amid systemic safety scrutiny and recent high-profile operational crises in the Indian aviation sector.

articles.headline: IAF to Acquire Ghatak Stealth Drones in ₹39,000 Crore Deal
articles.summary: The Indian Air Force is set to procure over 60 Ghatak unmanned stealth fighters under a new collaborative development model involving DRDO and private industry partners. The ₹39,000 crore project aims to build advanced drones capable of launching air-to-air and air-to-ground missiles. Induction is expected within eight years, marking a major shift toward unmanned aerial warfare.

articles.headline: IAF to Procure Two Mountain Radars in ₹1,950 Crore Deal
articles.summary: The Ministry of Defence has signed a ₹1,950 crore contract with Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) for the procurement of two mountain radars and related infrastructure for the Indian Air Force. These advanced radars are designed to enhance air defense and surveillance capabilities in high-altitude regions, strengthening national security along mountainous borders.

articles.headline: Bangladesh Foreign Minister Plans India Visit to Discuss Connectivity Projects
articles.summary: Bangladesh Foreign Minister Hasan Mahmud is scheduled to visit India next week to discuss progress on cross-border infrastructure and energy projects. Key agenda items include the renewal of the Ganges water sharing treaty and ensuring stable energy supplies. The visit comes amid regional concerns over fuel shortages and a desire to strengthen bilateral economic ties.

articles.headline: Gulf War Tensions Create Anxiety for Migrant Families in Kerala
articles.summary: The escalating conflict in West Asia has caused significant distress among families in Kerala who depend on remittances from the Gulf. With over 2.2 million Malayalis working in the region, concerns over job security, physical safety, and the potential halt of financial flows are high. The situation has become a key election issue, with voters seeking assurances on the safety of the diaspora.

articles.headline: Cops quiz man behind 1,100 fake bomb emails across India
articles.summary: Mumbai police are interrogating a 22-year-old postgraduate student for allegedly sending over 1,100 hoax bomb threats to schools, airports, and government offices across India. The suspect was traced using digital forensics after a series of threats caused widespread panic and flight delays. Authorities are investigating potential links to international hacker groups.

articles.headline: Engineer who lost US job ends life; techie wife jumps too
articles.summary: A young tech couple in Bengaluru died by suicide after the husband lost his job in the United States and was unable to find new employment. The 32-year-old engineer had recently returned from the US after a series of layoffs in the tech sector. His wife, who worked for an MNC in Bengaluru, reportedly followed him in the act out of distress.

articles.headline: Deadline day sees dozens of Maoists lay down arms
articles.summary: On the final day of a government-offered amnesty, dozens of Maoists surrendered across Chhattisgarh and Odisha. Officials described the move as a major success for the 'legacy and thrust' strategy to clear extremist-affected districts. The surrendering cadres will receive financial aid and rehabilitation as part of the government's surrender policy.

articles.headline: Crowd crush at temple in Bihar's Nalanda kills nine pilgrims
articles.summary: A stampede at the Sheetla Mata temple during the Maha Kumbh Mela in Nalanda, Bihar, resulted in the deaths of nine pilgrims, including eight women. The incident occurred when a large crowd attempted to enter the temple gates through a narrow corridor. The state government has announced an ex-gratia of ₹4 lakh for the families of the deceased.

articles.headline: Census 2027 to count population for first time with DigiPin
articles.summary: India's 2027 Census will debut 'DigiPin,' a digital addressing system developed by the Department of Posts. The system will provide every household with a unique 10-character code, ensuring precise location tracking and better delivery of social benefits. The move marks a significant shift toward a fully digital-first data collection process.

articles.headline: Blackstone plans $500 million IPO for healthcare billing firm AGS Health
articles.summary: Private equity firm Blackstone has hired bankers for a public listing of AGS Health, valuing the company at over $3 billion—double its acquisition price a year ago. The IPO is expected to raise $500 million through a mix of primary and secondary shares. AGS Health provides medical billing and coding services to major US hospitals and is benefiting from the boom in healthcare AI.

articles.headline: Mid-sized IT firms target Asia and Africa for growth beyond US
articles.summary: Indian IT firms like Persistent Systems, Hexaware, and Firstsource are scaling up operations in Asia-Pacific, West Asia, and Africa to reduce dependence on the US market. These regions now serve as both growth hotspots and strategic employee hubs. The shift aims to insulate businesses from macroeconomic tensions in the West and tap into emerging digital transformation demands globally.

articles.headline: UK’s National Grid to invest $400 million in India tech center
articles.summary: National Grid UK Ltd plans to spend up to $400 million to establish a global capability centre (GCC) in India. The facility, expected to be fully operational by 2032, will employ 1,000 people and handle system modelling and data management. This move follows a global trend of major utilities setting up back-end tech hubs in India to leverage the country's high-capability talent pool.

articles.headline: Easebuzz prepares for $100 million pre-IPO funding round
articles.summary: Digital payments platform Easebuzz has appointed Avendus to manage an $80-100 million pre-IPO funding round. The profitable fintech aims to set a valuation benchmark ahead of a public listing planned within 24-36 months. Easebuzz processed over $30 billion in gross transaction value in FY25 and reported a significant rise in net profit to ₹18.77 crore.

articles.headline: Oil price shock may drive consumer shift toward electric vehicles
articles.summary: The war in West Asia and rising fuel costs may accelerate adoption of electric vehicles in India. Pure-play EV makers like Ather Energy are seeing increased interest as the running cost of EVs becomes more attractive compared to petrol vehicles. Ather's retail sales are expected to grow over 40% in FY27, aided by government subsidies and the success of its new scooter platforms.

articles.headline: Kajaria Ceramics raises prices to protect margins amid gas supply issues
articles.summary: Tile maker Kajaria Ceramics has increased product prices by 7-8% to cushion its margins against rising input costs and gas supply disruptions in Morbi. While the company aims for 7-8% volume growth in Q4, incremental sales remain dependent on outsourced capacity. The management expects price hikes and cost-effective measures to maintain an operating margin of 17-18% in FY26.

articles.headline: Zetwerk files for $500 million IPO via confidential route
articles.summary: Manufacturing marketplace Zetwerk has filed preliminary papers with Sebi for an IPO to raise up to ₹5,000 crore. The company utilized the confidential pre-filing route, which allows for initial feedback without public disclosure. The offering is expected to include a fresh issue and an offer for sale by existing backers like Peak XV and Lightspeed as the firm eyes a 2026 listing.

articles.headline: Home appliances to cost 10% more due to rising input costs
articles.summary: Appliances like ACs and refrigerators are set for price hikes of up to 10% as the West Asia war drives up raw material and freight costs. Companies like Godrej and Hisense are monitoring the situation, with plastic and metal prices rising sharply. The disruption comes at the start of the crucial summer season, forcing manufacturers to balance margin protection with consumer affordability.

articles.headline: Sammaan Capital plans diversification into personal and gold loans
articles.summary: Sammaan Capital, formerly Indiabulls Housing Finance, is looking to diversify its loan book beyond mortgages. Following a ₹5,652 crore capital infusion from Abu Dhabi's IHC, the NBFC aims to reach 5 crore borrowers in three years. The strategy includes venturing into personal loans, gold loans, and MSME lending while leveraging IHC's majority stake to rebuild its franchise.

articles.headline: Rakul Preet Singh targets market gaps with wellness business investments
articles.summary: Actor and entrepreneur Rakul Preet Singh detailed her wellness-focused investment strategy, targeting gaps in the laundry, coffee, and nutrition sectors. Backing brands like 5àsec and Wellbeing Nutrition, Singh aims for profitable exits within five years. She emphasizes founder discipline and passion over formal business backgrounds, focusing on sectors where she can add direct value.

articles.headline: Indian consumer brands find starting easy but scaling increasingly difficult
articles.summary: While social commerce and creator-driven marketing make it easier to launch consumer brands, sustaining growth is becoming harder. Founders at the Mint Investment Summit highlighted the necessity of omnichannel distribution and quick commerce presence. Startups like SuperYou and Underneat are prioritizing brand visibility and tight SKU selection to navigate a competitive retail landscape.

articles.headline: Wow! Momo focuses on packaged foods to offset QSR margin pressure
articles.summary: Wow! Momo Foods expects packaged foods to contribute 15% of revenue by FY28 as it seeks higher-margin growth. The vertical already generates ₹100 crore in annual revenue through noodles and frozen momos. The company is delaying its IPO by two years due to market volatility, focusing instead on retail expansion and exports to West Asia to offset rising delivery commissions and costs.

articles.headline: Central Bank of India receives ₹296 crore tax demand notice
articles.summary: State-owned Central Bank of India has been served a ₹296.08 crore demand notice from the Income Tax Department for the assessment year 2024-25. The demand is related to alleged shortfalls in tax payments and specific disallowances. The bank stated it is in the process of challenging the order in appropriate legal forums, citing strong factual and legal grounds for its position.

articles.headline: India's diesel exports to SE Asia reach a seven-year high
articles.summary: Indian diesel exports to South-east Asia hit their highest level in seven years in March as traders filled short positions caused by the West Asia war. Reliance Industries accounted for 90% of the 1 million tonnes shipped. Refiners are capitalizing on higher spot margins in Asia by utilizing discounted Russian crude to replace disrupted West Asian supplies.

articles.headline: Lifelong Online targets ₹1,000 crore revenue through omnichannel execution
articles.summary: Consumer durables startup Lifelong Online has crossed the ₹1,000 crore revenue mark by expanding across marketplaces, quick commerce, and offline retail. The company focuses on value-driven product design and localized manufacturing. Despite a brief detour into a majority-stake deal with US-based Thrasio, the founders bought back control to focus on India's discretionary spending boom.

articles.headline: Industrialist Pawan Ruia arrested in multi-crore cyber fraud case
articles.summary: West Bengal police arrested industrialist Pawan Ruia for his alleged involvement in an interstate cyber fraud racket. The case involves the use of mobile apps to siphon off nearly ₹93 lakh from an elderly victim and hundreds of others across India. Investigations revealed that funds were laundered through shell companies and converted into cryptocurrency.

articles.headline: Novo Nordisk cuts Ozempic and Wegovy prices in India
articles.summary: Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk slashed prices for its weight-loss and diabetes drugs Ozempic and Wegovy by up to 48% in India. The move aims to counter competition from cheaper generics launched by local firms like Sun Pharma and Dr Reddy’s after the semaglutide patent expired. The lowest doses will now be priced starting at ₹1,415 per weekly shot.

articles.headline: West Asia war hits Firozabad's glass industry and exports
articles.summary: The glass manufacturing hub of Firozabad is facing factory closures and a 60% production drop due to gas supply disruptions and declining international orders. The industry relies on Regasified Liquefied Natural Gas from Qatar, which has been impacted by regional conflict. Manufacturers warned of a long-term impact on exports to the US, particularly for decorative items.

articles.headline: William Walsh named IndiGo Chief Executive Officer
articles.summary: Aviation veteran William Walsh has been appointed Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of InterGlobe Aviation (IndiGo), subject to regulatory approvals. He will step down as Director-General of the International Air Transport Association on July 31, 2026, and is expected to take charge by August 3.

articles.headline: RBI defers capital market exposure norms to July 1
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has postponed its revised capital market exposure norms to July 1, providing clarifications on acquisition financing and lending to market participants. The move aims to boost market confidence and liquidity.

articles.headline: IT sector set for modest Q4 FY26 amid deal pipelines
articles.summary: India's IT sector is poised for steady but modest Q4FY26 growth, with analysts citing selective demand and elongated decision cycles. Strong deal bookings and early AI opportunities support this outlook, though pricing pressures may cap expansion.

articles.headline: Switch Mobility tops FY26 electric bus market
articles.summary: Switch Mobility became India's leading electric bus maker in FY26, surging 177% to 1,144 units. This dethroned Tata Motors and Olectra Greentech, driven by strong uptake in State transport tenders.

articles.headline: Cargill India posts double-digit growth, B2B a key pillar
articles.summary: Global food corporation Cargill is witnessing double-digit growth in India, with its B2B business emerging as a key growth pillar. The company leverages its innovation centre to co-create solutions for evolving consumer needs.

articles.headline: Sula plans resort at Chandon site as biz hits ₹100 crore
articles.summary: Sula Vineyards plans a resort at its acquired Chandon winery in Nashik, as its wine tourism business crosses ₹100 crore revenue. This marks a significant expansion for the company's hospitality division.

articles.headline: E4W registrations up 88% in FY26
articles.summary: Electric four-wheeler (e4W) registrations in India surged 88% to 1.91 lakh units in FY26. Tata Motors retained market leadership but saw its share decline, while Mahindra Electric emerged as a strong challenger.

articles.headline: ACME signs ₹20,000 crore green ammonia deal with SECI
articles.summary: ACME Group signed a 10-year Green Ammonia Purchase Agreement (GAPA) with SECI for 370,000 TPA of green ammonia, valued at ₹20,000 crore. This demonstrates commercial viability of green ammonia production.

articles.headline: Export duties back: refiners' margins may come under strain
articles.summary: Standalone refiners face margin compression due to the re-introduction of export duties on diesel and ATF. The benefits from excise duty cuts may be offset by these taxes and increased LPG production mandates.

articles.headline: Trade remedies: vital tool to protect Indian firms
articles.summary: India's Finance Ministry rejected 85% of positive recommendations for trade remedies, raising concerns about a policy shift. Trade remedies are crucial for protecting domestic industries from unfair import practices.

articles.headline: SEBI allows mutual funds to keep retirement, children’s schemes alive
articles.summary: SEBI has reversed its decision to scrap retirement and children's funds, allowing them to continue. This provides relief to investors who would have faced significant tax liabilities if the schemes were closed.

articles.headline: Fund-raise via IPO hits another high of ₹1.79 lakh crore in FY26
articles.summary: Indian companies raised a record ₹1.79 lakh crore through mainboard IPOs in FY26, a 10% increase from the previous year. The IPO pipeline for FY27 remains robust, with over 150 companies looking to raise funds.

articles.headline: RoDTEP scheme for exporters extended till September 30
articles.summary: The government extended the RoDTEP scheme for exporters at fully restored rates until September 30, providing relief amid high freight costs and maritime disruptions. Exporters welcome the move but seek policy stability.

articles.headline: Insta-help model, urban demand drive investor interest in home services
articles.summary: App-based home services companies are attracting increased investor interest, with funding rising significantly. The shift towards on-demand services and urban market demand are driving growth in the sector.

articles.headline: Cocoa surges, raw sugar loses ground
articles.summary: Cocoa futures rose sharply, while raw sugar prices slipped from a five-month high. London cocoa futures gained 5.1% to £2,479 a tonne, driven by supply concerns.

articles.headline: Crude oil adds to record monthly gains amid tanker attack
articles.summary: Brent crude futures rose to $118.09 per barrel following a tanker attack in the Middle East and US warnings. This adds to the record monthly gains driven by geopolitical tensions.

articles.headline: Natural rubber zooms to ₹221/kg on tight supply, global cues
articles.summary: Natural rubber prices surged to ₹221/kg, driven by tight domestic and global supply, extreme temperatures, and rising crude oil prices. West Asia tensions also contributed to the price surge.

articles.headline: RBI continues trade relief measures amid W. Asia crisis
articles.summary: The RBI has extended certain trade relief measures until March 31, 2026, amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainties and logistical disruptions. This includes enhanced export credit periods and streamlined processing.

articles.headline: Brookfield India REIT Appoints Shashank Jain as New CEO and MD
articles.summary: Brookfield India Real Estate Trust has named Shashank Jain its CEO and Managing Director, effective July 1, 2026. Jain, currently a partner at PwC India, replaces Alok Aggarwal. The Reit is India’s only institutionally managed office Reit, overseeing 11 Grade A assets in gateway markets including Mumbai, Delhi, and Bengaluru.

articles.headline: Fintech Bachatt Raises $12 Million in Series A Funding Led by Accel
articles.summary: Savings and wealth platform Bachatt secured $12 million in a Series-A round with participation from Accel, Lightspeed, and Infoedge Ventures. The capital will be used to scale its existing savings solutions and develop new AI-driven wealth and credit tools for its merchant base, which includes 300 million self-employed individuals.

articles.headline: Bureaucrats on Indian Corporate Boards Hit 13 Year Low
articles.summary: The share of company directors with civil service backgrounds fell to 3.9% in FY26, down from 5.5% in FY13. The decline is sharpest in public sector companies, where absolute numbers fell from 148 to 120. Analysts cite the rise of new-age tech companies and more political appointments as reasons for the diminishing share of bureaucrats.

articles.headline: Essar’s Mesabi Metallics Secures $520 Million From Breakwall Capital
articles.summary: Mesabi Metallics has entered a $520 million senior secured credit facility with Breakwall Capital as it nears operations in Minnesota. The project involves a $2.5 billion iron-ore mine and pellet plant. The financing supports the production of DR-grade iron ore, helping US steelmakers reduce reliance on imported raw materials.

articles.headline: Signature Global and RMZ Seal ₹1,293 Crore Joint Venture Deal
articles.summary: Signature Global completed a ₹1,293 crore deal with RMZ Group for a 50% stake in its Gurugram Connectivity subsidiary. The 50:50 joint venture will develop a ₹14,000 crore mixed-use commercial project in Gurugram covering 3.94 million square feet. The project will include office spaces, hotels, and retail components on the SPR.

articles.headline: Airlines Quantify War Impact Impossible as Fares Rise: IATA
articles.summary: IATA Director General Willie Walsh stated it is impossible to quantify the full impact of the West Asia war on airlines, though fuel costs and airfares are already rising. India’s domestic passenger demand grew slightly by 0.6% in February. Total global demand rose 6.1%, but capacity deployment is being adjusted due to regional risks.

articles.headline: Manasum Plans ₹200 Crore Fundraise Targeting 2028 IPO
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based Manasum Senior Living plans to raise up to ₹200 crore to expand its managed portfolio to 10,000 units by 2028. The company is currently rolling out an "active senior living" project in GIFT City, Ahmedabad. The expansion targets senior executives approaching retirement who wish to remain professionally engaged in advisory roles.

articles.headline: Sammaan Capital Aims for Top 3 NBFC Status by FY29
articles.summary: Sammaan Capital plans to evolve into a diversified NBFC following an ₹8,850 crore investment from IHC. The firm aims to expand from four to over 15 product segments, including gold and business loans. It targets a ₹1.3 trillion AUM and 50 million customers by FY29, leveraging IHC's majority stake to support long-term liability management.

articles.headline: Warren Buffett Warns of Banking System Fragility and Interconnectivity
articles.summary: Warren Buffett cautioned that the banking system shows signs of fragility due to its connection with non-bank players. He stated that financial stability should be a priority for the Federal Reserve, as troubles can spread quickly across interconnected markets. He noted that in periods of panic, investors will likely still seek to exit quickly.

articles.headline: Active Mutual Funds Outperform Benchmarks on Risk Adjusted Basis
articles.summary: Analysis of three-year data shows 60-80% of active equity mutual funds generated positive alpha against benchmarks. Active smallcap schemes recorded the highest median alpha at 2%. Fund managers cite the flexibility to take large positions in attractive valuations during high volatility as a key advantage over passive strategies.

articles.headline: Wall Street Rises on Hopes for West Asia De-Escalation
articles.summary: US stocks rose on Tuesday following reports that President Trump is willing to end the military campaign in Iran even if the Strait of Hormuz remains partially closed. The Dow rose 0.9% and S&P 500 added 1.2%. However, Brent crude remains poised for a record monthly increase due to war-driven supply shocks.

articles.headline: Navi MF Pivots Toward Active Equity Space for Growth
articles.summary: Navi Mutual Fund is shifting strategy to offer active equity schemes, moving away from its previous focus on low-cost passives. CEO Aditya Mulki stated the firm has expanded management capabilities to target alpha opportunities for investors. The fund house’s AUM has grown to ₹9,200 crore, primarily led by its Nifty 50 Index Fund.

articles.headline: Sathya Agencies Files for ₹600 Crore Initial Public Offering
articles.summary: Sathya Agencies, a major South Indian consumer durables retailer, filed draft papers with Sebi for a ₹600 crore IPO. The offering consists of a ₹300 crore fresh issue and a ₹300 crore offer for sale by promoters. The company is capitalizing on strong demand for electronics and domestic appliances in the region.

articles.headline: LPG Supply Crisis Forces QSRs and Fine-Dining Chains to Halt Plans
articles.summary: Large restaurant chains like McDonald’s and Pizza Hut are shuttering some outlets due to severe LPG supply disruptions. The crisis, fueled by the West Asia conflict, has stalled expansion plans for major fine-dining groups. Some establishments are switching to induction or piped natural gas where possible, but mid-sized standalone restaurants are hardest hit as they rely on cylinders.

articles.headline: Corporate Insurance Premiums Plunge up to 80% Amid Intense Competition
articles.summary: Corporate insurance premiums have dropped by as much as 80% during this renewal season due to a surge in underwriting capacity and aggressive competition. Over 20 reinsurers have entered the Indian market, particularly at GIFT City, leading to a supply glut. While beneficial for corporate costs, experts warn that such sharp corrections might affect the long-term profitability of insurers.

articles.headline: Electric Vehicle Sales Surge in March with Record Two-Wheeler Registrations
articles.summary: Electric vehicle registrations hit record highs in March as consumers advanced purchases ahead of anticipated price hikes. Two-wheeler registrations jumped 36% to over 1.77 lakh units, while electric car sales rose 49% to nearly 19,711 units. The surge was driven by fear of fuel price increases linked to the West Asia conflict and the expiration of certain subsidy incentives.

articles.headline: China's Cosmx and Munoth Ink JV for Lithium-Ion Battery Sourcing
articles.summary: Chinese battery manufacturer Cosmx has signed a joint venture agreement with India’s Munoth Industries to source lithium-ion cells for the domestic market. The partnership will focus on supplying cells for smartphones and consumer electronics. Munoth will assemble battery packs using Cosmx technology, marking a significant step in localized electronics component manufacturing in India.

articles.headline: India's Metal Forming Market Projected to Reach $95 Billion by 2030
articles.summary: A report by Systematic Group estimates that India’s metal forming industry will grow at a 12% CAGR, reaching a valuation of $95 billion by 2030. The growth is driven by the booming automotive sector and a shift in global supply chains away from China. Increased private equity interest and M&A activity in the sector are expected to further fuel expansion and technological upgrades.
