articles.headline: Average Salary Hikes in India Likely to Reach 9.1% in 2026
articles.summary: A Deloitte report projects that average salary increments in India will reach 9.1% in 2026, slightly higher than the 9% recorded last year. The manufacturing and life sciences sectors are expected to lead with hikes of up to 9.8%. While IT and financial services may see more moderate increases, overall hiring sentiment remains strong as companies focus on talent retention and growth.

articles.headline: Consumption Slowdown in Q4 Dampens Full-Year Sales Performance for Brands
articles.summary: Major consumer electronics and appliance brands reported a sharp slowdown in sales during the final quarter of the fiscal year. High inflation and rising fuel prices have led to reduced discretionary spending. While demand for essential goods remained stable, premium segments like high-end smartphones and luxury refrigerators saw a decline, leading companies to reassess their growth targets for FY27.

articles.headline: IndiGo picks former IATA chief William Walsh as CEO
articles.summary: IndiGo has appointed William Walsh, the former head of IATA and British Airways, as its new CEO. He succeeds Pieter Elbers, who resigned last month after a successful four-year tenure. Walsh is expected to lead the airline through its next phase of global expansion, including the integration of long-haul wide-body aircraft into its fleet.

articles.headline: RBI extends US tariff-led relief for Indian exporters
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has extended interest subsidy and credit relief for exporters affected by US tariffs and geopolitical tensions in West Asia. The move is expected to provide liquidity to MSMEs facing delayed shipments and rising insurance costs. Exporters welcomed the decision, stating it would help them remain competitive in a volatile global market.

articles.headline: New rules may soon allow pharmacies to sell loose medicine tablets
articles.summary: India's drugs regulator plans to mandate that pharmacies dispense the exact number of tablets prescribed, ending the practice of forcing patients to buy full strips. The move addresses public grievances regarding medicine wastage and high costs. While health experts and patients welcome the reform, chemists have raised concerns about inventory management and missing batch details on cut strips.

articles.headline: Novo Nordisk slashes Wegovy and Ozempic prices in India by 50%
articles.summary: Novo Nordisk has reduced the starting dose prices of Ozempic and Wegovy by up to 48% in India following the loss of patent exclusivity on March 20. Monthly costs for starting doses will drop to ₹5,660. The move aims to maintain market share as cheaper generic versions enter the Indian market, which has high rates of obesity and type-2 diabetes.

articles.headline: Novo Nordisk Slashes GLP-1 Drug Prices to Compete in India
articles.summary: Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk has significantly reduced the prices of its GLP-1 drugs, including Ozempic and Wegovy, in the Indian market. The price cuts of up to 48% are intended to help the company retain its market share against emerging generic competition and improve affordability for diabetes and obesity patients. The move follows similar strategic price drops by Eli Lilly.

articles.headline: Fire at Sion Hospital sparks chaos for over 300 patients
articles.summary: A fire broke out in a pathology laboratory at Mumbai’s Sion Hospital, leading to the urgent evacuation of over 300 patients, including those from the pediatric ward. No casualties were reported as hospital staff and fire brigades managed to douse the flames within an hour. The incident has raised questions regarding fire safety protocols in aging municipal hospitals.

articles.headline: Tracking health but losing mind? Experts warn of app burnout
articles.summary: Health professionals are warning against 'app burnout' caused by constant tracking of steps, calories, and sleep. Excessive monitoring is reportedly leading to increased stress and anxiety instead of improving health. Experts recommend setting realistic goals and taking regular breaks from digital health tools to maintain mental well-being.

articles.headline: Kolkata emerges as lifeline for pets from Bangladesh, North-East
articles.summary: Kolkata has become a major regional hub for advanced veterinary care, attracting pet owners from Bangladesh and North-East India. The city's hospitals offer specialized treatments such as dialysis, cardiology, and oncology for pets, which are unavailable in neighboring regions. The pet-care tourism industry in Bengal is now valued at ₹3,600 crore annually.

articles.headline: India mandates E20 petrol nationwide despite low vehicle readiness
articles.summary: Every petrol pump in India will now dispense fuel with 20% ethanol (E20). However, Vahan data shows that fewer than 30% of petrol vehicles registered in 2025 are ethanol-compliant. Owners of older vehicles report concerns over reduced fuel efficiency and engine wear. The rollout aims to cut crude import dependence amid global energy supply disruptions caused by the West Asia war.

articles.headline: IMD predicts stormy April with lower than normal peak temperatures
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecast that summer day temperatures (April-June) will likely remain normal or below normal across most of India. Intense thunderstorms are expected in April, which could damage standing crops. However, the agency warned of a 62% chance of El Nino emerging by June-July, which may negatively impact the monsoon and farm sector later in the year.

articles.headline: IMD issues rain and hail alerts for Karnataka
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department issued weather alerts for Karnataka, including an orange alert for Kalaburagi, Vijayapura, and Dharwad due to moderate to heavy rain and possible hail. A yellow alert was issued for Bengaluru, where thundershowers are expected. Wind speeds may reach 50 kmph, raising concerns about crop damage and waterlogging in low-lying areas.

articles.headline: El Nino threat looms over Indian farm sector
articles.summary: The IMD predicted an 80% chance of El Nino persisting through August-October, posing a threat to India's agricultural growth. While grain output was record-high last year, concerns remain over fertilizer supply due to the West Asian war. Historically, moderate to strong El Nino conditions have led to negative agricultural growth in 11 out of 15 years.

articles.headline: IMD forecasts more heatwave days in May-June
articles.summary: The IMD predicts above-normal heatwave days in parts of East, Central, and North-West India during April-June. April rainfall may exceed normal levels, but heat stress could impact key crops.

articles.headline: IMD Predicts Wet April and Development of El Niño After June
articles.summary: The IMD expects April temperatures to remain normal to below normal, with rainfall predicted at 112% of the long period average. While weather models suggest El Niño will develop after June, the Met department will provide specific details on its impact on the Indian southwest monsoon during its annual forecast in mid-April.

articles.headline: Australia Eyes 'Decarbonisation Deals' to Support Global Climate Action
articles.summary: Australia is proposing the creation of a 'Decarbonisation Deals Platform' to facilitate international collaboration on reducing fossil fuel dependence. The initiative aims to provide a framework for sharing clean technology and scaling up investment in green energy projects. Australia’s climate ambassador highlighted the need for structured global efforts to meet ambitious net-zero targets.

articles.headline: Rain damages crops on 64,573 hectares in Maharashtra
articles.summary: Unseasonal rainfall and hailstorms in March have damaged standing rabi crops and fruits over 64,573 hectares across 28 districts in Maharashtra. Farmers have reported significant losses in wheat, grapes, and mango crops. The state government has ordered an immediate assessment of crop damage to provide timely compensation to the affected farmers.

articles.headline: Major bureaucratic shuffle: New DGCA chief and I&B secretary named
articles.summary: The government appointed Vir Vikram Yadav as the new Director General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) amid a sensitive time for the sector. Yadav, a 1996-batch IAS officer, replaces Faiz Ahmed Kidwai. Additionally, Abhishek Singh moves to the National Testing Agency, and Chanchal Kumar has been named secretary in the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting.

articles.headline: Centre to introduce bill designating Amaravati as sole Andhra capital
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah will introduce the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation (Amendment) Bill, 2026, in Parliament. The legislation aims to formally designate Amaravati as the state capital, providing statutory clarity by amending the 2014 Act. The bill is proposed to have retrospective effect from June 2, 2024, following a resolution by the state assembly.

articles.headline: Himanta promises strict expulsion laws against 'Bangladeshi Miyas' in Assam
articles.summary: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma vowed to stringently use the Illegal Immigrants Expulsion Act, 1950, to deport undocumented Bengali-speaking Muslims. Releasing the BJP's 31-point manifesto, he promised to implement the Uniform Civil Code within three months of returning to power, while exempting tribal areas. The party also pledged to create one million jobs over five years.

articles.headline: BJP Kerala manifesto promises free LPG and monetary aid
articles.summary: The BJP released its manifesto for the Kerala assembly polls, promising two free LPG cylinders annually and a monthly ₹2,500 'Bhakshya-Arogya Suraksha' card for poor households. Other key pledges include a monthly social welfare pension of ₹3,000, 20,000 litres of free water per year, an AIIMS for the state, and a high-speed railway network between Thiruvananthapuram and Kannur.

articles.headline: Karnataka Finance department flags fake memo on staff termination
articles.summary: The Karnataka Finance department lodged a police complaint regarding a fake memo circulating on social media. The fraudulent document claimed that the services of contract government staff would be terminated upon tenure completion. Officials clarified that no such memo was issued and urged the public to ignore the misinformation which has caused widespread confusion.

articles.headline: DK Shivakumar claims Congress wave in Davanagere South bypoll
articles.summary: Karnataka Congress chief D K Shivakumar stated that an internal party survey shows 79% support for the Congress in the Davanagere South by-election. The seat became vacant after the death of Shamanur Shivashankarappa. Shivakumar expressed confidence in victory despite a multi-cornered contest involving 14 candidates from the Muslim community and a challenge from the BJP.

articles.headline: Siddaramaiah projects Congress victory in Bagalkote by-election
articles.summary: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah expressed strong confidence that Congress candidate Umesh Meti will win the Bagalkote assembly seat. Addressing a rally, he described the late MLA HY Meti as a respected leader. Siddaramaiah dismissed BJP claims that his extended campaigning reflected fear of defeat, asserting that the public response across the constituency has been very positive.

articles.headline: Congress labels April 1 'Day of Loot' over inflation
articles.summary: Congress leader Randeep Singh Surjewala attacked the Centre, alleging that April 1 has become a 'day of loot' due to price hikes on 900 essential drugs, toll charges, and fuel. He claimed that the BJP is using inflation to burden the common man, noting that medicines for diabetes and hypertension would become costlier and highway toll collections in Karnataka have reached ₹23,000 crore.

articles.headline: Karnataka minister hospitalised in ICU for lung infection
articles.summary: State planning and statistics minister D Sudhakar was admitted to a private hospital in Bengaluru after his health deteriorated due to a lung infection. Officials in the Chief Minister’s Office reported fluctuations in his condition following respiratory problems. He is currently receiving treatment in the Intensive Care Unit.

articles.headline: Tejasvi Surya slams free IPL ticket quota for Karnataka MLAs
articles.summary: BJP MP Tejasvi Surya criticized the Karnataka government's decision to provide three free IPL tickets to every MLA for matches at M Chinnaswamy Stadium. Deputy CM DK Shivakumar defended the move, stating it followed legislator demands and stakeholder meetings. Surya labeled the decision as 'VIP culture' that mocks the needs of ordinary citizens.

articles.headline: BJP seeks to address Kerala church concerns over FCRA bill
articles.summary: The BJP's Kerala unit is holding talks with senior clergy to pacify church groups opposing the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act amendment bill. The Catholic Bishops Conference of India termed the bill 'dangerous', fearing assets could be arbitrarily seized. BJP leaders assured that the bill only targets fraud and that minority interests will be protected.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry signs ₹1,950-crore deal for mountain radars
articles.summary: The Ministry of Defence signed a ₹1,950-crore contract with Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) for two indigenous mountain radars for the Indian Air Force. Developed by DRDO, the radars are intended to strengthen air defences and reduce dependency on foreign equipment. This marks the final capital acquisition contract for the 2025-26 financial year.

articles.headline: Lt Gen Dhiraj Seth named Vice Chief of Army Staff
articles.summary: Lieutenant General Dhiraj Seth will take charge as the Vice Chief of the Army Staff, replacing Lt Gen Pushpendra Singh. Seth, an armored corps officer, was previously heading the Southern Command. The move is part of a top-level reshuffle in the Indian Army. Seth will be the senior-most officer when the current Army Chief retires in June.

articles.headline: TVK chief Vijay booked for election code violations in Chennai
articles.summary: Actor-politician Vijay and members of his party, Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, were booked by Chennai police for allegedly violating the Model Code of Conduct. The charges include causing public obstruction and using excessive sound systems during a roadshow. Vijay is making his political debut in the 2026 assembly polls, contesting from two constituencies.

articles.headline: Mamata Banerjee alleges BJP enrolling 'outsiders' as Bengal voters
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee wrote to the Chief Election Commissioner alleging that the BJP is attempting to enroll non-residents from other states as voters. She claimed thousands of Form 6 applications were submitted in a single day as a 'mischievous ploy'. The BJP countered by accusing Banerjee of using inflammatory rhetoric to intimidate voters.

articles.headline: Historical Nawab family names deleted from West Bengal voter rolls
articles.summary: Descendants of Mir Jafar, the 18th-century Nawab of Bengal, found their names removed from the electoral roll during a special revision in Murshidabad. Family members, including an 82-year-old descendant, were flagged for 'logical discrepancies' despite providing documentation. Local BJP officials stated the deletions were not intentional or politically motivated.

articles.headline: Nadia BDO suspended after violence at election training venue
articles.summary: The Election Commission ordered the suspension of a Block Development Officer (BDO) in West Bengal's Nadia district following violence at a poll training site. An FIR has been lodged and departmental proceedings initiated against the officer for a lack of seriousness in ensuring a secure and transparent election environment.

articles.headline: Florida Governor signs bill to rename airport after Trump
articles.summary: Governor Ron DeSantis signed a bill to rename Palm Beach International Airport as 'President Donald J. Trump International Airport'. The name change still requires administrative approval from the Federal Aviation Administration to update navigational charts and databases. The move honors the former president in his home state of Florida.

articles.headline: AI powers political war rooms for Tamil Nadu Assembly polls
articles.summary: Political parties in Tamil Nadu are leveraging AI for campaign strategies, from generating digital content to analysing public sentiment. AI tools are speeding up tasks like creating posters and understanding voter data.

articles.headline: Shifting rivalries sharpen contests across Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Assembly elections feature dense, high-profile contests due to evolving DMK-AIADMK rivalries, BJP's expanding footprint, and actor Vijay's debut. This has turned many seats into multi-cornered fights.

articles.headline: Piyush Goyal accuses DMK govt of ruining Tamil Nadu finances
articles.summary: Union Minister Piyush Goyal accused the DMK government of financial mismanagement and corruption, warning of increasing debt. He claimed the State's finances were ruined and electoral promises unmet.

articles.headline: NDA reveals Kerala manifesto with welfare, infra push
articles.summary: The NDA in Kerala released its manifesto promising ₹3,000 monthly pension for women heads, a high-speed rail corridor, and an AIIMS. The focus is on balancing welfare with infrastructure development.

articles.headline: Sabarimala row takes centre stage in Kerala polls
articles.summary: The Sabarimala issue has resurfaced as a political flashpoint in Kerala elections, with allegations of mishandling the shrine's reconstruction and gold theft. It remains a key factor for voters in south-central Kerala.

articles.headline: Soon, a law to formally declare Amaravati as capital of AP
articles.summary: The Centre will introduce a Bill to amend the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act, formally declaring Amaravati as the State's sole capital from June 2, 2024. This fulfills a long-standing demand from the TDP.

articles.headline: PM Modi: India Effectively Managed West Asia Crisis via Foreign Policy
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi stated that India has managed the West Asia conflict's impact through strong foreign policy and national unity. Speaking in Gujarat, he noted that despite global fuel price hikes, the government protected citizens from the burden. He also inaugurated Kaynes Semicon’s plant, positioning India as a global chip supplier.

articles.headline: Government Approves Major Bureaucratic Reshuffle Across Key Ministries
articles.summary: The Centre approved a reshuffle of top bureaucrats, appointing new secretaries in Information & Broadcasting, Tourism, and Minority Affairs. Chanchal Kumar becomes I&B Secretary, while Bhuvnesh Kumar takes over at Tourism. Abhishek Singh was named director general of the National Testing Agency, and Vir Vikram Yadav became the new DGCA chief.

articles.headline: BJP Promises ₹5 Trillion Infrastructure Push for Assam
articles.summary: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman released the BJP manifesto for Assam, promising a ₹5 trillion infrastructure investment and the implementation of the Uniform Civil Code. The 'Sankalp Patra' also pledges to recover encroached land and protect indigenous heritage. In Kerala, the party promised two free LPG cylinders annually and a new AIIMS.

articles.headline: Shipbuilding Cooperation Focus for South Korea President Visit
articles.summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung will visit India mid-April to deepen ties in shipbuilding and semiconductors. India aims to become a top 10 global shipbuilder by 2030 through Korean technology partnerships. The visit follows the elevation of ties to a 'Special Strategic Partnership' and targets building integrated maritime clusters.

articles.headline: BJP Promises UCC and Anti-Infiltration Laws in Assam Election Manifesto
articles.summary: Releasing its manifesto for the Assam assembly polls, the BJP has promised to implement a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) and stricter anti-infiltration laws. The party also pledged to create 2 lakh government jobs and provide land rights to indigenous people. The manifesto focuses on preserving local identity while vowing to detect and deport illegal immigrants using updated technology.

articles.headline: Bill to Name Amaravati as Andhra Pradesh Capital Reaches Lok Sabha
articles.summary: The Central government is set to introduce a bill in the Lok Sabha to amend the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act to officially identify Amaravati as the state's capital. This move follows a long-standing demand from the state government. The amendment will clarify that Hyderabad is the capital of Telangana, while Amaravati will serve as the new permanent capital of Andhra Pradesh.

articles.headline: Mamata Banerjee Flags Concerns Over High Volume of Voter Form Entries
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee has raised alarms over the submission of a large number of 'Form 6' applications for voter inclusion, alleging a plot to influence rolls. She warned that legitimate citizens should not be disenfranchised and called on the Election Commission to intervene. The BJP has dismissed the claims, stating the applications are part of routine voter enrollment drives.

articles.headline: BJP vows to expel illegal immigrants in Assam manifesto
articles.summary: The BJP released its manifesto for the upcoming Assam elections, pledging to implement the Immigrants (Expulsion from Assam) Act, 1950, to deport illegal migrants. The party also promised to protect indigenous culture, land, and heritage, and announced a ₹5 lakh crore boost for regional connectivity and infrastructure development.

articles.headline: Pinarayi pulls out of debate, draws sharp Congress criticism
articles.summary: Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan withdrew from a scheduled face-to-face debate with Opposition leader VD Satheesan. The CM's office cited logistical issues and a busy campaign schedule, but the Congress accused him of being 'afraid of accountability.' The cancellation has become a major talking point in the state's ongoing political campaign.

articles.headline: I&B Secretary among 12 new top-level bureaucratic appointments
articles.summary: The Union government announced a major reshuffle, appointing 12 new secretaries and 27 additional secretaries. Key changes include new heads for the Ministry of Information & Broadcasting, Finance, and Civil Aviation. The reshuffle is seen as an effort to streamline administration and accelerate project implementation ahead of the next fiscal year.

articles.headline: Eutelsat negotiates with Isro for future satellite launch options
articles.summary: European satellite provider Eutelsat is in discussions with the Indian Space Research Organisation (Isro) for future launches. Seeking to diversify beyond SpaceX and Ariane rockets, Eutelsat aims to bolster its launch capabilities. While negotiations are ongoing, a final deal has not been reached. The move highlights India's growing role as a competitive hub for global satellite missions.

articles.headline: India's R&D innovations face market hurdles due to lack of validation
articles.summary: Manufacturing executives at the Mint India Investment Summit noted that while R&D spending is rising, innovations struggle to reach the market without policy backing and assured demand. Companies like Borosil and Anupam Rasayan highlighted that a lack of global precedent often slows domestic adoption of new technologies. A collaborative ecosystem between industry and academia is needed.

articles.headline: IMD forecasts frequent heatwaves for east and central India through June
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department predicts that parts of east, central, and north-west India will experience more heatwave days than usual between April and June. Maximum temperatures are expected to be above normal in these regions. However, overall country-wide rainfall for April is likely to be above normal, except in the North-east which may see below-normal precipitation.

articles.headline: Domestic green ammonia supply to fertilizer firms delayed until 2028
articles.summary: Fertilizer companies like IFFCO and Coromandel have signed 10-year contracts for green ammonia, but actual supplies will only begin after three years. Producers need time to construct manufacturing plants under the National Green Hydrogen Mission. Industry executives noted that these agreements offer no immediate relief to the supply crunch caused by the ongoing West Asia crisis.

articles.headline: India Bioeconomy Projected to Reach $300 Billion by 2033
articles.summary: India’s biotech sector is expected to grow from $195 billion in 2026 to nearly $300 billion by 2033, according to Endiya Partners. The report highlights India's biopharma moment as global patent cliffs totaling $300 billion loom by 2030, positioning the country at an inflection point for drug development and innovation geography.

articles.headline: AERB Grants Approval for Excavation at Raj N-Project Unit One
articles.summary: The Atomic Energy Regulatory Board (AERB) has granted permission for the start of excavation for Unit One of the Rajasthan Atomic Power Project. The project is being developed by Anushakti Vidhyut Nigam, a joint venture between NPCIL and NTPC. Each unit at the site will have a 700 MWe pressurized heavy water reactor, part of India's plan to expand its indigenous nuclear power capacity.

articles.headline: Brain implant allows ALS patient to speak through synthetic voice
articles.summary: In a medical breakthrough, a brain-computer interface (BCI) has allowed a patient with ALS to communicate using a synthetic voice. The implant translates neural signals into text and speech with 90% accuracy. Researchers state that this technology could revolutionize communication for millions of people with severe speech and motor disabilities.

articles.headline: Succession planning crucial to protect beverage recipes and brand IP
articles.summary: Business owners can preserve secret recipes and brand identities through formal estate planning using trademarks, copyrights, and trade secrets. While India lacks specific legislation for trade secrets, they are protected under common law and contracts. Registered trademarks and designs are assignable and can be bequeathed via a will to ensure a secure transfer to the next generation.

articles.headline: IBBI suspends Supertech insolvency professional for two years
articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) suspended the registration of Hitesh Goel for two years following disciplinary findings. Lapses were identified in the corporate insolvency resolution processes of Supertech Ltd and Nobility Estates Pvt Ltd. The findings included failure to disclose material information and failure to convene the first Committee of Creditors meeting.

articles.headline: Uttarakhand CM orders crackdown after retired brigadier's killing
articles.summary: Chief Minister Pushkar Singh Dhami directed a state-wide crackdown on criminals following the death of a 74-year-old retired Army brigadier in Dehradun. The officer was killed by a stray bullet during a high-speed car chase between rival groups. Police launched 'Operation Prahar' to improve law and order, and four suspects have been arrested so far.

articles.headline: Newlywed woman booked for abetting husband's suicide in Mangaluru
articles.summary: Mangaluru police registered a case against a woman for allegedly abetting the suicide of her husband. The victim died after consuming poison following alleged sustained harassment by his wife. The case was filed based on a complaint by the victim's father. The victim passed away on March 29 after undergoing 12 days of medical treatment.

articles.headline: Ex-employee arrested for murdering manager at Bengaluru firm
articles.summary: A 30-year-old former employee, Hanumanthappa, was arrested for allegedly stabbing a manager to death at Power Plastic Private Limited in Bengaluru. The victim, Rahul, suffered 10 stab wounds during the attack at the company's premises. Police stated the accused had recently been terminated from his job and the attack occurred after a verbal altercation.

articles.headline: Rapido bike taxi rider arrested for molesting passenger
articles.summary: A 22-year-old Rapido rider, Mohammed Khaja, was arrested for allegedly molesting a 25-year-old woman during a ride in Bengaluru. The victim alleged the rider touched her inappropriately several times. The accused was remanded to judicial custody for 14 days. Rapido stated it has removed the rider and is cooperating with the police investigation.

articles.headline: Student arrested for abusing and filming five-year-old girl
articles.summary: A 19-year-old student was arrested in Hyderabad for allegedly sexually abusing a five-year-old girl and uploading the footage online. The incident was detected by the Telangana Cyber Security Bureau through a child abuse material tipline. The minor victim was rescued, and investigations revealed the abuse may have been unknown to her parents.

articles.headline: Kerala High Court upholds amendments to Lok Ayukta Act
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court upheld the state government's amendments to the Kerala Lok Ayukta Act, 1999. The court ruled that the changes do not violate the doctrine of separation of powers or judicial independence. The decision dismisses challenges that argued the amendments undermined the basic structure of the Constitution.

articles.headline: Odisha teen fleeing abuse gang-raped; two adults arrested
articles.summary: A 17-year-old girl who fled sexual abuse by her stepfather in Andhra Pradesh was allegedly drugged and gang-raped in Berhampur, Odisha. Police arrested two men and detained a minor boy in connection with the crime. The victim was rescued after locals alerted police. A separate case of abuse has been registered against the stepfather in Andhra Pradesh.

articles.headline: Kidney transplant racket exposed in Kanpur; five doctors arrested
articles.summary: Kanpur police arrested five doctors and a broker, uncovering an illegal kidney transplant racket that allegedly performed 50 transplants. The racket came to light after a donor from Uttarakhand reported a payment dispute. The accused allegedly used a Telegram group to connect donors and recipients, charging around ₹10 lakh per transplant.

articles.headline: Holistic approach needed on menstrual leave
articles.summary: The Supreme Court declined a blanket directive on menstrual leave, urging policymakers to balance social protection with economic opportunity. Experts note potential impacts on hiring and career progression for women.

articles.headline: ITR forms notified for AY27, experts advise careful scanning
articles.summary: Taxpayers can start filing AY27 income tax returns from Wednesday, with seven forms notified. Experts advise careful book scanning due to wider scope for ITR-1 and detailed declarations required in ITR-2, 3, and 4.

articles.headline: Bombay High Court Halts GST Action on Hotel Restaurant Levy
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court stayed a ₹4 crore GST show-cause notice challenging the 18% tax on restaurants located within hotels charging high room tariffs. The petitioner argued that linking tax rates to room tariffs rather than the nature of the restaurant service lacks a rational nexus. The court maintained the status quo pending a June hearing.

articles.headline: Sebi Bars Elitecon and Promoter Over Alleged Financial Fraud
articles.summary: Sebi barred Elitecon International and its MD Vipin Sharma for alleged price-volume manipulation and misleading financial disclosures. The regulator flagged a correlation between revenue spikes and falling electricity use. Sebi ordered the impounding of ₹51.3 crore in illegal gains and froze connected bank accounts pending a forensic audit.

articles.headline: Transgender Amendment Bill Gets President Assent Amid Welfare Concerns
articles.summary: The Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill 2026 has become law, removing self-identification rights and introducing a narrower definition. Only 6.8% of India's transgender population holds TGID cards. While ₹250 crore has been allocated for welfare since FY22, actual spending stands at only ₹13 crore amid bureaucratic delays.

articles.headline: Hague Court to Rule on Pakistan's Plea Against India's PCA Suspension
articles.summary: The Permanent Court of Arbitration at The Hague is set to rule on Pakistan’s request for interim measures against India's suspension of PCA proceedings. India has refused to recognize the court's jurisdiction over the Indus Waters Treaty dispute, citing illegalities. The court will decide whether to halt progress on India's Kishanganga and Ratle hydroelectric projects pending a final verdict.

articles.headline: Transgender bill gets President's nod despite ongoing protests
articles.summary: President Droupadi Murmu has given her assent to the Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, 2026. The new law removes the provision for self-perceived identity, requiring certification from a medical board. Activists have expressed concern over the loss of autonomy and have vowed to challenge the constitutional validity of the amendment in court.

articles.headline: Judge upholds woman's right to promote her religious beliefs
articles.summary: A Mumbai sessions court set aside a magistrate's order against a woman accused of promoting her religion. The judge ruled that the Constitution grants every person the freedom to promote their faith. The court found the lower court had wrongly relied on a police report without proper evidence, emphasizing that sharing religious texts is a protected fundamental right.

articles.headline: HC appoints amicus curiae to study 50-year age cap for IVF
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has appointed an amicus curiae to examine the constitutional validity of the 50-year age limit for women seeking IVF under the ART Act. The move comes after petitions challenged the cap as unconstitutional and discriminatory. The court highlighted the lack of analytical data behind the current age restrictions for assisted reproduction.

articles.headline: Kurla bus crash driver gets bail after 11 months
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court granted bail to Sanjay More, the driver of a Kurla BEST bus involved in a fatal crash that killed nine people and injured 37. The court noted that the prosecution failed to prove criminal intent, and the driver had already spent 11 months in custody. More had claimed a mechanical failure caused the bus to accelerate uncontrollably.

articles.headline: HC permits 15-year-old to terminate pregnancy after 28 weeks
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court permitted a 15-year-old sexual assault survivor to terminate her 28-week pregnancy. Overturning a medical board report that warned of risks to the fetus, the court prioritized the girl's mental health and physical well-being. The judge noted that the trauma of carrying the pregnancy would cause irreparable damage to the minor.

articles.headline: Women writers dominate International Booker Prize shortlist
articles.summary: Organisers announced the shortlist for the International Booker Prize, featuring six novels primarily by women writers. The list includes works about an exiled Iranian family, a suburban French witch, and an Albanian sworn virgin. The £50,000 prize will be awarded in London on May 19, recognizing fiction translated into English from around the world.

articles.headline: Praggnanandhaa suffers first loss to Sindarov in Candidates tournament
articles.summary: Indian Grandmaster R Praggnanandhaa suffered his first defeat in the Candidates tournament, losing to long-time rival Javokhir Sindarov of Uzbekistan in Round 3. Sindarov's victory placed him in a co-lead with world No. 3 Fabiano Caruana. In the women’s section, Bibisara Assaubayeva defeated top seed Zhu Jiner, while India’s Divya Deshmukh managed a draw.

articles.headline: World champion Gukesh to skip major events to regain form
articles.summary: Chess world champion D Gukesh announced he will compete with less intensity and skip several long-distance tournaments following a string of disappointing performances. The 19-year-old aims to focus on dedicated training to regain his best form. He will participate in only two European Rapid & Blitz events this year, with the support of tournament organizers.

articles.headline: Cooper Connolly leads Punjab Kings to victory on IPL debut
articles.summary: Young Australian Cooper Connolly scored an unbeaten 72 off 44 balls to guide Punjab Kings to a three-wicket win over Gujarat Titans. Chasing 163, Punjab overcame a mid-innings wobble as Connolly anchored the chase in the closing stages. Earlier, Punjab's bowlers, led by Vijaykumar Vyshak's 3/34, restricted Gujarat to a modest total of 162/6.

articles.headline: Ryan Williams makes history in India's win over Hong Kong
articles.summary: Ryan Williams scored the fastest-ever debut goal for India, netting just four minutes into the Asian Cup qualifier against Hong Kong in Kochi. India secured a 2-1 victory with a second-half volley from Akash Mishra. This marked the first win for head coach Khalid Jamil and India's first victory in seven matches since early 2025.

articles.headline: FIFA chief Infantino attends Iran-Costa Rica friendly in Turkey
articles.summary: FIFA President Gianni Infantino attended a friendly match between Iran and Costa Rica in Antalya, Turkey. The match took place amid diplomatic tensions regarding Iran’s participation in the 2026 World Cup hosted in North America. Players wore black armbands to honor victims of a recent school strike in southern Iran.

articles.headline: Semenya Vows to Fight IOC's New Gender-Screening Policy for Athletes
articles.summary: Double Olympic champion Caster Semenya has criticized the IOC's new policy on gender testing for female athletes with DSD (Differences of Sexual Development). Semenya argued that the rules are discriminatory and lack adequate consultation with affected athletes. She has vowed to continue her legal battle to ensure that natural biological variations are not used to exclude women from competition.

articles.headline: Punjab Kings win thriller against Gujarat Titans in IPL
articles.summary: Punjab Kings secured a thrilling three-wicket win over Gujarat Titans in their latest IPL encounter. Chasing a target of 199, Punjab reached the total with one ball to spare, thanks to a stunning 72-run debut performance by their Australian middle-order recruit. The victory keeps Punjab's playoff hopes alive in a highly competitive season.

articles.headline: Govt extends zero duty import of tur, urad; retains 30% levy on yellow peas
articles.summary: The government extended duty-free imports for tur and urad until March 31, 2027, ensuring adequate supplies. Yellow peas will continue to attract a 30% import duty.

articles.headline: Edtech UpGrad Plans to Reach 50 Offline Centres Within 12 Months
articles.summary: UpGrad aims to double its offline presence to 50 centres by the end of FY27. The firm is set to open three new locations in Panipat, Siliguri, and Chandigarh this month. Currently operating 23 centres, the company is targeting Tier-II and Tier-III markets where individuals aged 21-26 are seeking job-ready technical and soft skills.

articles.headline: FYJC quota for girls may be hiked to 50 percent
articles.summary: The Maharashtra school education department is considering increasing the quota for girls in First Year Junior College (FYJC) admissions from 30% to 50%. The move aims to improve female access to higher education and encourage continuation of studies. The proposal would remove the current cap in in-house quotas for girl students in minority and non-minority colleges.

articles.headline: AI Startup Gnani.ai Raises $10 Million for Global Expansion
articles.summary: Indigenous AI startup Gnani.ai secured $10 million in Series B funding led by Aavishkaar Capital. The company plans to enhance agentic AI capabilities and expand multilingual voice solutions globally, strengthening its engineering and product talent base following the launch of its 5-billion-parameter voice model.

articles.headline: India's 5G Subscriber Base to Exceed 1 Billion by 2031: Nokia Report
articles.summary: A Nokia report projects India's 5G subscriber base will surpass 1 billion by 2031, driven by rapid adoption and data consumption. 5G traffic grew 70% in 2025, accounting for nearly 47% of mobile broadband usage, with average data consumption reaching 31 GB per user monthly.

articles.headline: CBIC Removes ₹10 Lakh Value Cap on Courier Exports to Boost E-commerce
articles.summary: India removed the ₹10 lakh value limit for courier exports from April 1, aiming to boost e-commerce for MSMEs. The CBIC also enabled returns of uncleared parcels, reducing logistics inefficiencies and enhancing global competitiveness for small exporters.

articles.headline: New Income Tax Act 2025 Simplifies Rules, Enhances Salaried Benefits
articles.summary: The Income Tax Act 2025, effective April 1, reduces rules from 511 to 333. It introduces a fixed 'tax year,' higher deductions for education/hostel allowances, a new share buyback tax scheme, and a voluntary disclosure for unreported foreign assets to ease compliance.

articles.headline: West Asia War Triggers Market Rout, Indian Equities Face Pessimism
articles.summary: The West Asia war drove Indian equities into oversold territory in March, with the Sensex dropping 7%. Nearly half of Nifty 500 stocks are near 52-week lows. FIIs sold equities worth ₹1.12 trillion, impacting the rupee and contributing to the worst performance in six years.

articles.headline: RBI Extends Enhanced Export Credit Relief Until June 30, 2026
articles.summary: The RBI extended the 450-day export credit window for disbursements until June 30, 2026, to ease liquidity for exporters facing geopolitical and supply chain disruptions. This crucial support aims to maintain trade continuity amid global uncertainties.

articles.headline: Mumbai Property Registrations Hit 14-Year High in March
articles.summary: Mumbai recorded 15,516 property registrations in March 2026, the highest for the month in 14 years, generating ₹1,492 crore in stamp duty. This growth, up 19% sequentially, was fueled by sustained end-user demand, particularly in the mid-income segment.

articles.headline: Stampede at Bihar Temple Kills Eight Devotees
articles.summary: A stampede at the Sheetla Mata temple in Bihar's Nalanda district killed eight devotees, mostly women, and injured eight others due to heavy crowds. Bihar CM announced ex-gratia payments, and an inquiry has been ordered into the incident.

articles.headline: Indian Express Articles Unreadable
articles.summary: The content of the Indian Express articles is unreadable due to severe character corruption. No specific news, facts, or figures could be extracted from the provided text.

articles.headline: IndiGo Appoints Global Aviation Veteran William Walsh as New CEO
articles.summary: IndiGo appointed William Walsh, former IATA head and CEO of British Airways, as its new CEO, effective August 3. Walsh succeeds Pieter Elbers and will steer the airline's expansion and operational recovery amid market challenges.

articles.headline: Mindspace REIT Acquires Chennai IT Park for ₹2,541 Crore
articles.summary: Mindspace REIT acquired a 2.6 million sq ft IT park in Chennai for ₹2,541 crore, boosting its portfolio to over 33 million sq ft. The board also approved a preferential issuance of up to ₹675 crore to fund the transaction and capture future leasing demand.

articles.headline: Unilever and McCormick Merge Food Businesses, Creating $65 Billion Giant
articles.summary: Unilever is merging its food business with McCormick in a $65 billion Reverse Morris Trust transaction, forming a major global food player. Unilever will hold a 65% stake and receive $15.7 billion cash.

articles.headline: Magnum Ice Cream Completes 61.9% Stake Acquisition in Kwality Wall’s India
articles.summary: Magnum Ice Cream Netherlands acquired a 61.9% stake in Kwality Wall’s (India) Ltd. from Unilever, changing the promoter classification. KWIL will remain listed, with a potential for the group to hold up to 75% via a tender offer.

articles.headline: Novo Nordisk Slashes Ozempic and Wegovy Prices in India
articles.summary: Novo Nordisk reduced Ozempic by 36% and Wegovy by 48% in India, preempting generic competition. The company has no plans to cut prices for its oral semaglutide, Rybelsus.

articles.headline: Vir Vikram Yadav Appointed DGCA Chief Amid Bureaucratic Reshuffle
articles.summary: Senior IAS officer Vir Vikram Yadav has been appointed Director General of Civil Aviation (DGCA). Chanchal Kumar is the new Secretary of Information and Broadcasting, as part of broader bureaucratic changes.

articles.headline: Tennis Star Leander Paes Joins BJP Ahead of West Bengal Elections
articles.summary: Olympic medalist Leander Paes joined the BJP, inspired by PM Modi's vision. Previously with TMC, he aims to empower youth through sports infrastructure. His induction is seen as a move to attract young voters.

articles.headline: Ashwini Bhide Becomes Mumbai's First Woman Municipal Commissioner
articles.summary: Ashwini Bhide, who led major infrastructure projects like Metro 3, is appointed Mumbai's first woman Municipal Commissioner in its 160-year history. She faces the challenge of managing the city's ₹80,000-crore budget.

articles.headline: NASA Begins Countdown for First Manned Lunar Launch in 53 Years
articles.summary: NASA has started the countdown for its first manned lunar mission in 53 years, with the SLS rocket carrying four astronauts. This historic flight will test systems for future lunar and Mars exploration.

articles.headline: Sebi Bars Elitecon International and Promoter for Market Manipulation
articles.summary: Sebi barred Elitecon International and its MD from securities markets for five years due to manipulative trading and misleading disclosures, ordering disgorgement of ₹51.26 crore in unlawful gains from 'pump and dump' schemes.
